text: This way.
Come on, hurry up.
Better to stick together down here.
Whew. That was close.
Sneaking is overrated, eh?
Hmm. That doesn't go anywhere.
No going back that way, now.
That thing was a dragon. No doubt. Just like the children's stories and the legends. The harbingers of the End Times.
Almost there.
I'd rather not tangle with her right now. Let's try to sneak by. Just take it nice and slow, and watch where you step.
That looks like the way out! I knew we'd make it!
The ivory from this tusk will be perfect for the inlay on my amulets.
When Elgrim said we were setting up shop down here, I told him he would catch his death.
The dirt, the moisture... it's disgusting. Who wants to spend their life living like a skeever?
But that old bellyacher just fed me some nonsense about light ruining his mixtures and being close to the water... agh.
It's about time; I sent them a letter weeks ago. That Elgrim... he'd forget his own pants if his wife didn't help him dress.
Here you go. Tell Hafjorg that she can chip off whatever she thinks is a fair trade for their time.
Did you ever have to fight off bandits, Frida? Like in your stories?
Leigelf is a good man, and I'm not running out on him now.
You mean Hildolf the Skull-Splitter? Remember when we rounded up the townspeople and dragged him out of that cave?
Why, I once fought alongside your father when we were both younger. Saved him from getting his fool head sliced off.
Oh, got a bit of an interest in your boss do you?
Those were the days. I'd never seen my husband fight so bravely before.
Who were you fighting against?
Aye, to your husband. Divines bless his memory.
No, not that it's any of your business.
Oh, he was young, and handsome. I had half a mind to go after him, myself, before I met my husband.
Oh, dearie, it was so long ago. But I do remember the celebration we had afterwards. Mead never tasted so good.
Did I ever tell you about the day when Skald the Elder was Skald the Younger?
Thoring, do you remember the time when that wolf pack come clamoring into town?
Tough day at the mines. I swear the ore is getting harder to dig.
I can't imagine Skald ever being young.
Frida, you aren't saying....
How could I forget? That old alpha male almost took off my leg.
You're just getting older.
Never did find out why they came sprawling into town like that.
I was a beauty in my day, dearie. Don't act so surprised.
That's why I come here to talk to you. You're all heart.
Animals in the Pale don't need a reason to attack Dawnstar. It's just their nature.
Dawnstar has always held its own against the bandits and wild animals of the Pale.
You should quit working in the Eastern Mine. Leigelf's no good to work for.
Now, who was it that kidnapped the Jarl's son 20 years ago?
I swear, if I wasn't around, Elgrim would forget to eat his meals. How we make any coin at all is beyond me.
In fact, I need to arrange for someone to pick up an ore sample for me in Shor's Stone... goodness knows Elgrim won't lift a finger to do it.
Thank you, marsh-friend.
Thank you, land-strider.
Be careful, the road to Shor's Stone could be dangerous.
Don't forget us if you need supplies for your journey.
Auxiliary.
Quaestor.
Praefect.
Tribune.
Legate.
It's always good to see someone I can count on.
The general expects so much with so little.
The sooner we end this rebellion, the sooner I'll be able to sleep at night.
Ulfric... what have you done my old friend?
Stormcloaks... even the name of their rebellion stinks of Ulfric's vanity. He wants to be king. That's all this is about. One man's ambition.
I don't fight for the glory of the Empire, nor do I glory in the death of my kinsmen. I fight to bring peace and order to my homeland.
Damnit! Why can't Ulfric see he's tearing Skyrim to pieces?
Sometimes I wonder if the gods have abandoned us... as we have abandoned them.
What I do, I do for Skyrim and her people.
When we destroy the rebellion, perhaps the Thalmor will call off their damned inquisitions. It's just making things worse.
Sometimes I miss being out there in the middle of the action.
Unblooded.
Ice-Veins.
Bone-Breaker.
Snow-Hammer.
Stormblade.
Always good to see someone I can trust.
Sometimes I wonder if Ulfric understands what I'm dealing with out here.
That godless Emperor can shove his damn treaty into the steaming nether-reaches of my you-know-where.
My hatred for the Empire is exceeded only by my hatred for their witch-elf puppet-masters.
What will Skyrim have become if her children submitted to tyranny?
The Empire would have us become their slaves as they have become slaves to the Thalmor. I will not live as a slave, nor will I die as one.
The Empire is weak. That is why we fight them. Skyrim must be ruled by the strong.
Some say Ulfric desires to be king and nothing more... have they not seen that his anger floats on a sea of tears?
Countless dead haunt countless sleepless nights... when Skyrim becomes whole again, perhaps so will I.
When we've reclaimed our homeland from the Imperials, we'll take the fight to the Thalmor. They are the true enemy. Make no mistake.
If there was someone else Ulfric could trust with all this planning nonsense, I'd be out there breaking heads with the rest of you.
Buy it? This? An Elder Council amulet?
Oh yes. Oh yes, indeed.
Wait just one moment...
Here. It's a letter of credit. Usable, by Astrid only, for any service or item I can provide. As per our standard arrangement.
You bring that back to your lovely mistress. With my regards.
The Elder Council...
Oh, now that explains quite a bit. Motierre, you naughty, naughty boy. Hiring the Dark Brotherhood to help you rise beyond your station. Delicious.
Was Mallory willing to buy the amulet?
Let's see...
Where oh where did you get this? Don't answer - I don't want to know.
This is an amulet of the Emperor's Elder Council. Specially crafted for each member. Worth a small fortune. Ain't somethin' you'd give up lightly.
Look, it ain't my business ta tell the Dark Brotherhood its business, but if you killed a member of the Elder Council, you'd better belie-
Good, you're back. Well, what did Mallory have to say? Is the amulet authentic?
Oh. Oh I see.
Well, you're makin' friends all over, ain't ya?
So, uh... how is Astrid doing' these days? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
Ah, but business! Of course. What kind of business?
Oh. Oh I see.
Well now, how is Astrid doin' these days, uh? Tell her to stop by some time. We can have a drink. Catch up.
Ah, but we can discuss that later, yeah? What does the Brotherhood need?
Somethin' ya need? Anything for the guild.
Ah, now you must be lost. Best ya scurry off while you're able. The Ratway, well, it has a habit of swallowin' up the uninvited.
Poor Cicero. Dear Cicero. Such a humble servant. But he will never hear my voice. For he is not the Listener.
I knew I could count on you.
Good. Don't die out there.
We all have faith in you.
Go with the Nine.
Talos keep you.
Don't screw this up.
Good. And remember - keep the gods, and they'll keep you.
Excellent. Put the fear of the Nine into those motherless dogs.
That's what I wanted to hear. Good luck out there.
Good luck out there.
We do this for the good of Skyrim and her people. Never forget that.
Make me proud.
Rexus. The items.
Oh, Rexus is no mere servant. He has been with the Motierre family since I was a child. I trust the man with my life.
I have vowed to serve Amaund Motierre until my dying breath. Best remember that.
Here.
May the gods keep you.
Stay sharp out there.
Good. Make it it happen, soldier.
The whole of Whiterun is ripe with paranoia and tensions. The Jarl's court is right to fear the power I hold behind this door.
The Jarl trusts few, and they will be his undoing. The dark child knows of what I speak.
Let him guide your path.
Show the rebels the futility of their cause.
As long as the rebels are willing to die fighting the Empire, we must be willing to oblige them.
Peace may yet return to Skyrim. But until then, we fight.
I hope you're just playing dumb. I'm the one who left the note in Ustengrav.
Yes. We're very old enemies. And if my suspicions are correct, they might have something to do with the dragons returning.
But that isn't important right now. What is important is that you might be Dragonborn.
Good. The whole point of being in hiding is to appear to be someone you're not.
Yes. Congratulations, you are now a full-fledged member of the Bards College.
Due to your adventurous nature several of the college professors have some things they wish you to do.
What's more, Elisif has declared the Burning of King Olaf should become a weekly event.
And finally, there's the matter of the Patronage that Elisif wanted me to give you.
Not many come to see me. What do you want?
I know many things. I have studied things beyond the reach of most humans, traveled the Oblivion planes, seen things one should not see.
I have met Daedra and Dwemer and everything in between and I know enough to see a vampire where others would see a man.
Great. Looks like you'll be one of us soon.  We'll gather outside the College for the Burning of King Olaf.
Ah the bard-to-be. Did Viarmo send you?
Could you... could you give her this letter, and say it's from Sven? I think I've matched that Nord's lack of cleverness perfectly.
I appreciate your help. Please, take this. Some gold I've saved up from working at the mill.
What is that blowhard up to now?
So, he thinks he can turn Camilla against me? Two can play at this.
Here. Give this letter to Camilla and tell her it's from Sven. Let's see what she thinks of him after reading that.
You may be right. Camilla may see Faendal as little more than a friend, but why take chances?
Here. Give Camilla this letter. It's full of venomous nonsense. Tell her it's from Faendal.
I've been thinking... maybe Camilla needs a little help seeing Sven for what he is.
Camilla Valerius knows I'm the best man in Riverwood. That elf is kidding himself if he thinks she would choose him over me.
I've seen him sneaking over to the Riverwood Trader to speak with her when I'm not around. He's wasting his time.
Is that sarcasm? I've heard better wisecracks from Orgnar.
Still, you have a point. Camilla letting Faendal visit her isn't a good thing for me.
Here, let me give you a particularly venomous letter. Say it's from Faendal. That should get Camilla to stop inviting the elf over.
He's a bard, so he says. Occasionally he finds time to do his job here at the mill.
Thinks his ballads and sonnets are going to convince Camilla Valerius to marry him. As if she would say "yes."
An intelligent, beautiful woman like her wouldn't fall for that nonsense... I hope.
I will be back at your house if you need me, Thane.
(whine)
Looks like you've already got someone with you.
Ready to get going?
Mara protect us both.
This is your initiation into the coven. Go ahead. Have the first bite.
I don't need your jokes right now, but maybe you have a point. Maybe Camilla needs some help seeing Sven for what he is.
Tell me as soon as you've given Camilla the letter.
That letter ought to convince Camilla to never speak to Faendal ever again.
Faendal thinks he can woo Camilla Valerius away from me. She's already mine, I keep telling him.
Another poem, I'll bet. He does know how to make a girl blush.
What's this? If that oaf thinks all I'm going to do is stay in that filthy house of his and clean, I'll....
You can tell Sven that he already has a mother. I'm not speaking to him anymore.
He what? What are you talking about?
Why that jealous idiot. Thought he could just manipulate me into never seeing Sven again?
Thank you for telling me the truth. You should speak to Sven. I'm sure he'll want to thank you, too, for defending his name.
What is this? The elf playing at treachery? Shocking.
As if I would write this dribble. How about we play a bit of turnabout with Faendal?
Here. I had to use simple words, but this letter should convince Camilla it's from him.
From Faendal? That's strange. He's never written to me before.
What, what is this? Is this what he really thinks about me?
Well, you can tell that... that long-eared ass not to come around the store anymore. He's not welcome.
Now that's what I like to hear. Good work. I think you deserve some gold from my tips at the inn.
As you will, my Thane. I will protect you with my life.
What are you talking about? What's in the letter?
Oh my. He... he wanted me to think Faendal wrote this?
Thank you for telling me the truth. Could you talk to Faendal, as well?  I'm sure he'll want to thank you for standing up for him.
As you wish, my Thane.
(growl)
Yeah!
A dragon is attacking the Western Watchtower.
Now what do you say? Shall we go kill us a dragon?
That's a fair question. None of us have ever seen a dragon before, or expected to face one in battle.
But we are honorbound to fight it, even if we fail. This dragon is threatening our homes... our families.
But Housecarl... how can we fight a dragon?
You heard right! I said a dragon!
Here's the situation.
Yeah!
Damn right!
I don't much care where it came from or who sent it. What I do know is that it's made the mistake of attacking Whiterun!
They are rare things, I have one I picked up during my research of the Oblivion realms but it is a precious item.
I met several of your kind during my studies of life-extending magics. I even  considered becoming a vampire myself.
In the end, vampirism would endanger my ward Agni which would defeat the intended purpose.
It requires a filled black soul gem. You will need to kill someone.
When you have a gem and have filled it return to me and I will perform the ritual. I will bring life to your dead body, vampire.
They say Falion of Morthal has studied vampires, draugr and all matter of undead.
Very good. I hope this will allow us to maintain our reputation.
You've been suspended from the College for what you did. You'd better talk to Tolfdir if you want to get it sorted out.
I'm sorry, but you've been suspended from the College. I can't be seen talking to you right now.
I would hate to see our reputation further tarnished.
"Because Solitude would not soon bend knee, Olaf would hurt them while his status accrued."
"He sacked Winterhold in dragon form, and bent their minds to blame Solitude."
Ah, Viarmo.  I assume you are here to petition for the reinstatement of the Burning of King Olaf Festival.
I am, Jarl. I wish to present King Olaf's verse from the Poetic Edda. Recovered this very day from the Bards Tomb.
Ah, you mentioned something that would convince us the festival should take place but I didn't expect King Olaf's lost verse. Please proceed.
Well done. They never knew what hit them.
Now let's keep moving.
You have your orders. Get moving.
We had the advantage of surprise this time, but don't expect it to last. Let's move out.
Take that crown back to Solitude, soldier.
Get the crown already. We don't need to hang around here any longer than necessary.
The crown should be around here somewhere. Spread out, and keep your eyes open.
Enough, Maramal. We've all heard of the dragons and their return. There's no need to use them as an excuse to harass our customers.
Put down your flagons filled with your vile liquids and embrace the teachings of the handmaiden of Kyne.
Very well, Talen. I'll remove myself from this den of iniquity.
No, no... Maramal... we talked about this. Talen...
People of Riften, heed my words. The return of the dragons is not mere coincidence.
We're not kicking you out, just keep the sermons at the temple and let us all sin in peace.
Keerava, certainly we can come to some sort of an understanding. These people must be made aware of the chaos they've sown.
This is one of the signs. The signs that Lady Mara is displeased with your constant inebriation.
It's the Imperials! Take cover!
Quick, I can cut you loose inside the keep.
It's the escaped prisoners!
Get this gate open.
She what?
Oh, I should've known she'd try it again!
Thanks for warning me. I'll see to it this is taken care of.
Well, I suppose I could use a hand with something, yes.
Faralda's working on something, and I want to know what it is.
I want her notes. You think you could get them for me, without her knowing?
I'm still waiting for those notes, if you can get them.
Yes, yes it is.
You have a problem with that?
Look, either you get the notes or I'll figure out another way to do it. Just come find me if you can get your hands on them.
Good, good. She didn't see you take them?
Al right, this should be enough to figure out what she's up to. Then I can make sure it doesn't work.
Good work. Here, take this, and keep quiet.
I'll throw some money at you. Good enough?
Just get the notes and bring them to me. If you can't do it, I'll find some other way to manage it.
You're the one that got out of Helgen, aren't you? I could use anyone who's tangled with a dragon before.
Where do you think you'll be the most help - scouting ahead or supporting the rest of my men?
Well? You going to scout ahead or travel with me?
Jarl Balgruuf is waiting.
No, and I'm not expecting any.
J'zargo continues to struggle with wards. You have time to practice?
My family didn't really approve of me coming here at all.
No, not really. Couriers don't seem to like delivering letters here.
Ah, that's too bad.
Have you heard from your family recently?
Still no word from your family?
J'zargo understands. You hesitate because you know J'zargo will be better than you. Very well.
Maybe later. I'm sort of busy now.
Oh. Well, that's... awkward. Sorry to have brought it up.
Not at all. It means I'm not being hounded about my progress at the College.
Really? That must be so nice. I came all the way to Skyrim just to get away from my family's expectations.
Damn thieves. As if things weren't bad enough.
I call upon Oblivion realms. The home of those who are not our ancestors. Answer my plea!
Very well. Meet me at the summoning circle in the marsh at dawn. We shall banish the creature you have become.
Stand in the center of the circle.
As in death there is new life, in Oblivion there is a beginning for that which has ended.
I would not sell it to just anyone but I'll make an exception for you. The cost to replace the gem is quite high.
You are human again. Like the rest of us.
The ritual is complete.
I call forth that power! Accept the soul that we offer!
Good, you're here.
That may be true, but I ordered Irileth to bring back proof. You'd best report back to her at once.
There's no refusing a summons from the Greybeards. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your power.
So what happened at the watchtower? Was the dragon there?
Help Irileth kill this dragon before it can attack Whiterun. There's no time to lose.
Not now.
You sure took care of that dragon. Dragonborn or not, that's good enough for me.
I'm proud to be your battle-brother, Dragonborn. Whiterun doesn't need to fear with you on our side.
If you really are Dragonborn, then Skyrim needs you. Go to High Hrothgar and talk to the Greybeards.
Good enough. We'll meet up near the watchtower. Don't take any chances until we get there.
That was the hairiest fight I've ever been in, and I've been in more than a few.
I don't know about this Dragonborn business, but I'm sure glad you're with us.
You better get back to Whiterun right away. Jarl Balgruuf will want to know what happened here.
I'm taking command here for the moment. You head back to Whiterun and let the Jarl know what happened.
We'd better go make sure that bastard is really dead. Collect us a trophy or something to bring back to Ulfric.
Scatter and get behind cover! We need to hurt it, somehow! If you've got a bow or spells, now would be a good time to use them!
(Heavy breathing)
We stand at the tomb of Hevnoraak, who has been dead for many generations. I fear, however, that his return may be at hand.
You would do this? Three vessels in the tomb below hold the power to vanquish Hevnoraak. Before anything else, we need those.
Bring them to me - but be careful, mortal. They are guarded by Hevnoraak's minions. They will stop at nothing to see their master return.
Leave, stranger. Evil stirs in this place. I fear for the security of the very land if it should break free.
You've found the vessels. I worried that I had sent you to your death.
I have been holding him here. While he regains strength, however, I am fading.
By now I can barely contain his power, much less defeat him if he awakens. But with the help of a living champion I may be able to succeed.
I'm just as puzzled as you, lass.
Aye, there will be blood today. I can promise you that.
Crime is one thing, murder is another. Mercer will answer for this.
I'd just assume die before I'd let Mercer get those gems.
Do the Falmer also have no sense of smell? This place reeks!
The Eyes would go a long way in helping get the Guild back on its feet.
I can't wait to get my hands around that bastard's neck.
By Talos, Mercer has a lot to answer for.
With any luck, this will be Mercer's final day alive.
This place, it's like a maze. How did the dwarves find their way around?
This is just like old times.
Keep as quiet as you can. The Falmer may be blind, but they can still hear us.
If Mercer leaves here with the Eyes, we'll never see him again.
We have to get the Key back.
Mercer's here. I can feel it.
Be careful, there's no telling what lurks within these walls.
There's little time for talk, what do you want?
Yes, yes, what is it now?
You're quite noisy for someone who claims to be a thief.
Must you keep stopping?
What? What is it?
If you see Karliah, don't hesitate. Kill her.
That's enough of this unnecessary chatting, we need to keep going.
Let's get going. I want her head on my mantle.
Have your weapon at the ready. I'm certain Karliah knows we're here.
Keep your eyes open and your mouth shut.
Quickly, what is it?
As always. The Nords don't ever stop wanting things enchanted.
Why, because I'm a Dunmer? We didn't all grow up in dwarven ruins, you know.
Perhaps, but it just means more work for me.
I've had yet another complaint. Please stop barging in on experiments in progress.
Do you think you might, err, have a moment to look at something for me?
Yes, I just thought perhaps you'd have, err, come across something like this before.
Ugh. What do you want?
Don't even talk to me, Gane.
I'm busy right now, Colette.
Keeping busy, Sergius?
Well, then. Fine. Have a nice day.
Hello, Nirya.
At least it's more money for the College.
My dear, I am merely observing the proceedings.
Some days I marvel that you haven't managed to blow yourself up.
Well then "observe" from a greater distance. You're making people uncomfortable.
If this is another dwarven trinket, I keep telling you I'm not familiar with them.
Ah, err, yes. Sorry.
What are you up to, Gane? Hmm?
Why should I want anything? Why can't I simply be sociable with my colleagues?
How are you, Drevis?
Well, err, yes. Sorry to have troubled you.
What? Err, nothing!
No, my lord. It was just circling overhead when I left. I never ran so fast in my life... I thought it would come after me for sure.
I've already ordered my men to muster near the main gate.
One last thing, Irileth. This isn't a death or glory mission. I need to know what we're dealing with.
Irileth, you'd better gather some guardsmen and get down there.
Good. Don't fail me.
Good work, son. We'll take it from here. Head down to the barracks for some food and rest. You've earned it.
Yes, my lord.
You think so? Let's see who the real woman is here.
What news? Why isn't Fjola with you?
I hope my son has found some nice, well-mannered girl to settle down with.
Damn soldiers always trample my crops.
My husband just gets cranky when he doesn't get enough sleep.
I've lived through rebellion, Forsworn, undead, witches, wizards, hagravens, and marriage.
We're getting back to work as best we can.
Any day now more draugr are going to come out of that crypt.
This is the bridge out of town. The path up the mountain to the northwest leads to Bleak Falls Barrow.
I guess I should get back to my brother. He'll throw a fit if I take too long. Such a child....
I... I'm sorry Haelga, I can't. Not yet.
I'll pay you back, if it means that much.
I'm telling you, Vulwulf, it's the investment you've been waiting for.
That old place? I could care less about it. Its you I want, Bolli.
The only reason you have the gold for your "business" is because I gave it to you.
If you want to go out to Whiterun and burn that meadery down, go right ahead.
And when the guard traces it back here, and Maven is implicated, I'll be sure to tell her it was your idea.
The Dragonborn is said to be uniquely gifted in the Voice - the ability to focus your vital essence into a Thu'um, or Shout.
The Greybeards are masters of the Way of the Voice. If you really are Dragonborn, they can teach you how to use your gift.
There's no question about that. It was a mighty deed. You've earned a place of honor among the heroes of Whiterun.
But there must be more to it than that. Did something... strange... happen when the dragon died?
I knew I could count on Irileth. But there must be more to it than that.
So it's true. The Greybeards really were summoning you.
We were just talking about you. My brother needs a word with you.
The Greybeards...
Dragonborn? What do you know about the Dragonborn?
You heard the summons. What else could it mean?
Not just the men. The Greybeards seem to think the same thing.
Didn't you hear the thundering sound as you returned to Whiterun?
That was the voice of the Greybeards, summoning you to High Hrothgar!
This hasn't happened in ... centuries, at least. Not since Tiber Septim himself was summoned when he was still Talos of Atmora!
Masters of the Way of the Voice. They live in seclusion high on the slopes of the Throat of the World.
I told you, I can't just pick up and leave. I have my wife, my business... not to mention you have the Bunkhouse.
Come on, think about it. She'd reward us!
When are you going to take me away from here Bolli?
If you think I'd would make a move like that without asking Maven, you're out of your mind. Doing that would be a fatal mistake.
Is it really me, or is it your wife? You need to make a choice Bolli. The honey or her.
Don't worry, I'll treat those shipments as if I owned them myself.
So, what do you think of my plan. You in or out?
Haelga! That's just it. I can't keep up with you. Your Dibellan techniques... they're exhausting.
It's only because I've had an excellent teacher.
Shor's Beard! You're right! I'll do it... provided you supervise the shipments personally.
Aerin, you never cease to amaze me. You always see the good in everything.
Oh, Bolli you silly man. You weren't saying that when you were licking that honey off my...
Exactly. What better way to help the cause than to put weapons into the hands of young Nord warriors dying for their homeland?
I think some of them do, Lady Mjoll. They're just too frightened to step up and do something about it like you are.
Haelga, we need to stop seeing each other.
Sometimes I'm not certain that the people of Riften want to be helped.
It's not about the coin, it's about honoring your legacy by picking up arms and keeping this land free.
The sooner you remember that, the better.
I'm sorry you disapprove, father. But I've made up my mind. I'm not a warrior, I'm a businessman.
So I pay for the weapon shipments and you oversee their distribution to the Stormcloaks?
If you want to die, so be it.
We weep.
We shout.
Did he now? I can only hope.
You still have the witches' heads? Excellent. Throw one of them into the fire. It will release their magic, for me at least.
We weep.
And for ourselves...
We shout.
That's the spirit. I'll get all the details from Galmar when he gets back. Now then...
The blood of our fallen heroes will make Skyrim free, never doubt it. Also, never doubt me or Galmar. Now then...
Damn it. What were they doing there? Imperial spies are everywhere. Never forget that...  I trust you gave them a thrashing? Now then...
Gretta, Engar, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Imperial reinforcements taking us by surprise.
The rest of you lot are with me.
You two, stay and guard the entrance. We don't want any Stormcloak reinforcements taking us by surprise.
Everyone else, with me.
What? You have it?
No... No, as tempting as it is, it won't do to have both sides angry at us.
The College has had enough trouble staying out of the war. No need to make it worse.
You take that book to General Tullius or Ulfric and let them have their little triumph. It will be delivered back here to me soon enough.
We grieve.
We weep.
We weep.
We shout.
We take our leave.
We take our leave.
We take our leave.
Look around, see if you see anything unusual. Any signs of the mound being disturbed. Anything.
We hurt them in the past, and they have long memories.

If that's what she wants, then that's what she'll get.
There is one... Yngvar. He's quite popular with the ladies.
Thankfully Faleen is not quite his type, but they've been friends for some time, and he may have some ideas.
Please, ask him what she likes. It's my only chance.
Wait... you mean I should just... tell her? Tell her how I feel?
The dawn surely opens upon you, child. You must bear its light that all may see.
Helping out Ainethach, huh? Give me a reason not to plunge a dagger in your chest.
I want you sellswords out of my mine.
No. This is my land. You expect me to just hand it over to those Silver-Blood thugs?
You haven't heard of the Silver-Blood family? They're the most important people in the Reach.
They practically own the city of Markarth. And we're here on their coin.
Forsworn attacked a while back. Jarl had no men to spare, so the Silver-Bloods generously offered to send us to clean things up.
We just want that old native to hand over his land in payment for our services. Then we'll leave, and everyone can get back to work.
Watch your tongue, native. We'll leave when we're sure there's no Forsworn here.
Oh, and when would that be, I wonder? When I sell my land to the Silver-Bloods?
That won't be the last we hear from the Silver-Bloods, but at least we can get back to work.
Thank you. You've done a good thing for me and my workers. Here. This is for you.
A family of thieves. Running their corrupt empire from Markarth.
The Silver-Blood family own half the Reach, and they won't stop until they own all of it.
Robbery is what it is. The Silver-Bloods are trying to muscle in on my land.
Forsworn attack the mines, and suddenly all these sellswords show up to "help." They won't let anyone work until I sell it off.
The Silver-Bloods have made you a very generous offer for this pile of dirt. I suggest you take it.
The mine is closed. Get lost.
I've been sent by Silver-Bloods to keep this mine "safe."
Our business is done here.
Are you all right, dear? I thought I saw a tear in your eye a moment ago.
What I meant to say was... I love having an Orc here in the barracks. They're so... pleasant.
Nothing, Ragnar. Please... don't hurt me.
I've been thinking, would you like to join me in hunting?
We're doomed. The mine is going to stay closed, and we'll up be carted of to Cidhna Mine as slaves.
I can afford to buy meat now. I prefer working in the mines, anyway.
Oh, I'm fine, Enmon. I just... I still miss her.
Why do your people claim the Reach is theirs? The Orcs have been in these lands just as long.
But why did they take her? What could they want with our little girl?
Only criminals get sent to Cidhna Mine. They're not going to send us there.
Tell me, Ragnar, what do you think of the Orcs?
You wouldn't understand, Lash. Your people aren't smart enough for politics.
I... I'm sorry, Belchimac. I didn't mean that.
You want me to hunt with you? I thought you preferred being alone.
I miss her, too, but I'm glad she's safe.
Oh, they'll find some way to send us there. It'll be just my luck.
You have strong arms. You'd make a good Orc.
Is that so? I'll remember that next time we're in the mine tunnels... alone.
Your arms and legs are fit for catching strong, agile prey. I could use your help.
Cunning warriors and skilled workers. It's good to have you here, Lash.
You're always talking about the Forsworn. You aren't thinking of joining them, are you?
So am I. I hope she's happy at the temple.
I don't know. The guards are out looking for her, now. They'll be back soon.
Did... did I say you weren't smart? I meant to say that I'm not smart, Lash. I'm just not smart enough to answer your questions.
I'd be honored, but I have work to do in the mines.
Maybe I will. What do you think about that?
Um... thank you, Lash. You'd... make a fine Nord?
I asked what you thought of the Orcs, not about me.
You keep saying that. What if she's gone forever? What if they've done something to her?
Enmon, dear, do you have any regrets? About letting her go?
When will Sanuarach Mine be open?
Don't say words you don't mean.
I'm warning you. Those people are killers. You'd never last a day with them.
Oh. I'm sorry. Well, it's good to have you here, anyway.
Are you all right, dear?
No... no, we did the right thing. The temple will take good care of her, and one day she'll be helping all of Skyrim.
I don't like bunking with Orcs. It's not... clean.
You're right. She'll be a great Sybil, won't she?
Damn Nords. Always getting in the way of us honest folk.
I don't know. Whenever those Silver-Blood men decide to leave.
When are those Silver-Blood men going to leave?
The house is so... empty, Enmon... and quiet.
If you want me to leave, you could challenge me to a fight. I promise to stop once you've lost an arm.
What did you say? You lazy coward, I'll cut your throat.
I haven't seen you hunting in a while.
Never. They've shut us down forever, Enmon. The Divines have abandoned us.
Razelan. What are you doing?
Someone bring me a drink, quick!
Fine, fine. Get your hands off me. I'll be a good boy now.
Wouldn't want to offend our Thalmor overlords, would we?
Attention, everyone! Could I have your attention, please! I have an announcement to make!
I propose a toast to Elenwen! Our mistress!
I speak figuratively, of course. Nothing could be more unlikely than that someone would actually want her in their bed.
Although... most of you are already in bed with her! But again... I speak figuratively, of course!
Not lately. Why?
Just when you think things couldn't get any worse.
Of course. I rented a bed there. Where else would I sleep?
Sounds good. May I owe you for them?
What's that supposed to mean?
Good. You could be our first line of defense since you live outside the city walls, so we're depending on you.
Brand-Shei, did you hear about Helgen? It's... it's gone! A dragon burned it to the ground!
Grelka, have you had anyone come by and try to sell you an iron war axe?
Marise, I've come to warn you that the Thieves Guild has been harassing shopkeepers again.
So, Tythis... you coming by the Bunkhouse later?
I have two more bushels for you, Marise. That should keep you well-stocked.
If he paid what he owes, I wouldn't have to say anything. Besides, what in Oblivion do you care?
Yes, Lady Mjoll. If Riften gets attacked, we'll retreat inside the city gates and bar the door just like you said.
I'll see what I can do.
Religion isn't going to save me. So unless Mara is going to start raining coin from the heavens, why don't you mind your own business.
One of them came in and demanded payment. Bolli tried to tell them no; stood up to them, but...
Sigh. Yes, as usual. I'll be there.
Shadr, that's so sweet... I'd love to.
What's this nonsense I've been hearing? How dare you call Bolli a deadbeat!
Have you prepared the stables like I suggested, Shadr?
That's fine. I have a special occasion coming up, and I'd like to have it.
You poor thing. You should come by the temple, speak to Maramal.
Very well.
No, no... not at all. Mara believes that the spark of a union comes from inside us, not from our appearance.
Tell you what... if you still feel unsure, come by the temple sometime and we'll talk about it.
Did something happen at the Pawned Prawn?
Will we be dining together this evening after my work at the fishery?
Naw... he's fine. Look, I wanted to ask if you'll... um, if you'd like to... well, like to have a drink with me at the Bee and Barb sometime?
It's a religious thing.
Maven Black-Briar, the matriarch of the family, represents everything that's wrong with this city.
She's bribed countless officials, has friends back in the Imperial City and freely associates with the Thieves Guild.
I've tried everything I can to protect Riften's citizens from her family, but to no avail.
If I see him, I'll make sure he doesn't do anything brash. Don't worry.
The Argonian sugar-cane wine? It's pretty hard to get around here. Very expensive.
What's so good about it? I've got deadbeats who don't pay me, the city guard looking over my shoulder and Maven Black-Briar breathing down my neck.
Of course he wasn't you stupid whore; he's a man. Now I'm not going to warn you again. Is that clear?
There's someone I like very much in Riften. I think I'd like to marry him someday, but... well, he's not of my kind. Would Mara be angry?
I've come to warn you that the Thieves Guild seems to be back to its old tricks again.
Oh! Bolli... yes, hello.
I'm well. How are things over at the stables? Old Hofgrir giving you a hard time?
Try it out? What would you need a leather harness for anyway?
Looking for work?
It's Bersi. He's getting so angry about the corruption in Riften, he said he's going to walk right up to Maven and give her a piece of his mind.
Brand-Shei, do you think you can find me some theilul?
Good day, Grelka.
He wasn't protesting, Maven.
Feel free to ask me anything you like.
Why in goodness name would you have... oh never mind, just bring them by my house later and I'll see what I can do.
Nivenor, my darling. What a pleasant surprise.
Hi, Marise. How are you today?
Oh, good. I wanted to try that out as soon as possible.
What is it, Drifa? What's the matter?
Maven, I... please, don't hurt me.
Haelga, I like you, so I'm only going to say this one time.
If you ever try and "practice the Dibellan arts" with Hemming again, I'll make sure that pretty little face loses its beauty.
Horker skin, actually.
When I got close, the mercenaries Aringoth hired to protect the estate just waved me off. I don't think he wants visitors anymore.
I don't know. All we can do is hope the Eight will favor us and have these creatures stay far away from Riften.
Oh, Haelga... there you are. Your special order from Hammerfell came in off the last Khajiit caravan.
Mjoll! I'm glad I found you.
Turning him in to the guards? Have you joined Mjoll as official peacekeepers of Riften? You cost me a lot of coin.
Sigh.
Dinya? I was reading that missive you gave me and I was wondering something about marriage.
Leather? If they're armored, you should talk to Balimund over at The Scorched Hammer.
Why didn't you then?
Do you... do you think that could happen here? I mean, could a dragon come here and burn the city down?
Oh I get it. "It's missing so Grelka must have it." I wish this town would stop pointing their fingers at me.
There is a Thieves Guild in town you know.
I hope that doesn't mean things are going to get worse around here.
Maven, please. I didn't know it was your man. Had I known, I would have looked the other way, I swear.
Good, that must be very comfortable for you. I think mine's a bit too small. Perhaps we could trade?
You know I can't tell you that. As soon as I do, and you have a few meads in you, everyone will know.
Grelka, can you repair boots?
Bountiful. Although I would like to have anchored a bit closer to Goldenglow Estate.
Yes, I heard. I think all of Skyrim knows by know.
Well, we had one go missing from the Pawned Prawn, and I was just wondering...
I've heard. Bolli and Keerava were speaking of it just a little while ago.
I don't like being made a fool of, Brand-Shei. Not one bit.
I have plenty of room in my bed.
Of course.
Say, how are you going to sell all those fish before they spoil?
Oh come off it, Niv. We both know you married that fisherman for his gold. You don't need to put on an act for my benefit.
Hello Bolli. How's the fishing?
Aha! Here we are. Honored guests, I present to you - the Gourmet!
You, an assassin for the Dark Brotherhood, have just made an attempt on the Emperor's life. Would have succeeded, had it been the real man.
Quite. Yet that recent business with the young officer. Maro, was it? How dreadful. The son of your commander, plotting your assassination.
Surprised? So was I, when a member of your "Family" came to me with the plan. We worked out a deal, you see. An exchange.
Ah, the Potage le Magnifique. So delicious. My friends, as Emperor, I of course reserve the right of first taste.
Yes, an unfortunate turn of events, that. But an isolated incident. And I have been assured that the fault was with the man's son alone.
I get you, and the Dark Brotherhood gets to continue its existence.
Kill him. And make sure there's nothing left to bury.
Kill her. And make sure there's nothing left to bury.
By the gods! The Gourmet has killed the Emperor! Attack!
By the gods! The Gourmet and the chef have poisoned the Emperor! Get them!
You mean the wedding? My cousin's apparent murder? An unfortunate misunderstanding, no more. Cold mead, hot tempers... these things happen.
But you know what? I've changed my mind. How about this? I kill you, and butcher each and every one of your miserable little friends?
Have you found something?
Good, good. Does it say where he may be headed?
No, nothing. At least nothing I'm aware of.
Well... If I'm being honest, I haven't exactly been discreet lately in expressing my frustration with this whole situation.
Obeying the Night Mother. You being the Listener. It's ridiculous. No offense.
Cicero may have overheard me talking to one of the others about the Night Mother. It's possible I was... not entirely respectful.
But to go this far. To attempt to murder the leader of a Sanctuary. Cicero must pay with his life. There is no other option.
The Dawnstar Sanctuary? Whatever for?
Never mind, it doesn't matter. You need to leave. Now.
Every moment counts, so I want you to take my horse. His name is Shadowmere. You'll find him outside, by the pool. Let's just say he's... one of us.
Find Arnbjorn. Make sure my husband's all right.
And then, send that jester's twisted little soul to the Void, in as many pieces as possible.
But aren't even you the least bit nervous? After everything that's happened?
Truth is, we are in no danger whatsoever. Killing an Emperor can be useful, but befriending one? Now that's beneficial - as I'm sure you'd all agree.
(Clapping.)
Here we are. Gods, I'm nervous. We'll go in in just a moment. Please, I'll serve. You just stand there and... be amazing.
That man was, by far, the most insufferable decoy the Emperor has ever employed. I'm glad he's dead. Ah, but I'm even happier that you killed him.
Your Sanctuary's being put to the sword right now. That's what I think of this "deal." You killed my son! All of you! And now you'll pay the price.
Ugh... damn thing.
Just... stay back.
Back off.
Can't trust these things.
Beem! What are you doing?
I can't decide if things are about to get worse or better.
At least we don't have a dragon to worry about, for the moment.
No. I'm a Breton from High Rock, and I refuse to be mistaken for a filthy native.
I don't care if we share the same ancestors, these people are savages. Half of them are Forsworn barbarians, and have you seen what they eat?
What have I done for the gods to take me so far from my beloved Daggerfall?
We're the ruling body of the Aldmeri Dominion. Saviors of Mer. Victors of the Great War.
The Empire exists because we allow it to exist, and I'm here to make sure the Jarl of Markarth remembers that.
Damn Elves! You'll pay for this!
Praise Talos!
The Thalmor saved all of Elven-kind during the Oblivion Crisis. We've been watching over our lands for 200 years.
We re-founded the Aldmeri Dominion, an alliance between us and our Bosmer cousins. You might know them from their common name. Wood elves.
We intend to prove the superiority of Mer over Man, one century at a time.
Hmph. Fine.
Very well.
Ogmund the skald. He's old, respected, and I know for a fact that he worships Talos in his home.
But the Jarl has been hesitant to call for his arrest. I want you to break in to his home and find evidence.
Good hunting.
Ah, there we have it. This will be all I need to have Ogmund taken care of.
I would like to personally thank you on behalf of myself and the Thalmor for your assistance. Here, for your work.
Depends on what you need. A light armor is good for travel. Less to carry. A heavy armor weighs you down, but no true warrior goes without it.
If you want some heavy armor, I might have a deal we could work out. I need a Daedra's Heart. Jarl wants me to cool his new sword in blood.
You get me a heart, I get you some armor. Interested?
It's a religious matter. The Thalmor do not recognize Talos as a god. He was only a man, and does not deserve to a place in our pantheon.
The Empire has agreed to accept our beliefs, and its citizens have a responsibility to cease their heretical worship.
You're awfully inquisitive, aren't you? I like that. Perhaps you'd care to solve a little problem I'm having?
He's the real head of this family. He may not be the eldest, but all the work that keeps us respected is handled by my Thonar.
Anything. Served in the Legion with my sister, Ghorza, for over 10 years. We've seen every piece you can imagine.
Have you met her? She runs the forge near the smelter. Just don't bother talking to her apprentice. Boy has no talent.
As if this craggy, wretch of a city could give birth to a superiorly bred Mer such as myself. No, I'm not from Markarth.
I was sent here to lead the Thalmor's interests in this corner of Skyrim. It's my mission to root out all Talos worship in this city.
Good. I'd prefer using water to cool my metal, but it's what the Jarl wants.
Here. Some of my best armor. Wear it well.
Jarl can refuse to see me all he likes. He can't ignore us forever.
My husband Thonar handles all our family's business. He's an important man.
The Dominion is here, and we're watching you.
The Thalmor know how to deal with Talos worshippers.
Help us and you may earn a pardon.
Hey, weren't you on the carts with the Stormcloaks?
Ah, well, if Hadvar vouches for you I guess it must be all right.
I saw Caius.. that dragon just grabbed him and... I don't know how I made it out of there...
Nothing for it but to keep moving.
Better down here than out there.
A dragon.... First in a thousand years....
We need to find a way out of here.
What?
Worry about yourself.
Thank Talos you came along... I thought I was going to die down here.
Imperial dogs... there's nothing that's too low for them.
You were with us on the carts, weren't you? Not your lucky day, eh?
I wonder if there's any more Imperials hiding down here somewhere.
Ralof seems to like you. That's good enough for me.
What do you make of it... the dragon? What does it mean?
What next?
I hope someone knows where we're going.
Better down here than out there.
Just keep putting one foot in front of the other.
I think we're almost out. Come on!
Take it slow and careful, and I think we can sneak by her.
We must be near the entrance for that bear to make its den here.
I'll follow your lead.
I've got your back, don't worry.
Not now.
Biggest damn spiders I've ever seen.
The others'll have to find another way out.
This is the longest day I can remember.
Let's see if we can find a way out of here.
We'd better stick together down here.
I sure hope there's a way out somewhere down here.
Here's the noble Empire in all its glory. Pah.
Come on!
Look around. Grab anything useful.
There may be a way out, down this way.
We'd better get moving before any more Imperials show up.
Let's see where this goes. Better than going back outside with that dragon.
Get yourself a weapon. I'm going to need your help if we run into any more Imperials.
We've got to find a way out of here.
Don't worry. We'll get out of here, somehow.
I don't think this day is ever going to end.
Better in here than out there.
You're doing me quite a favor. I won't forget it.
I'm sure she'll be angry. Brace yourself.
Can't do that and keep an eye on things here at the same time.
But... You could take word to her in Solitude, let her know it'll be coming soon.
I'd make it worth your while. No one would notice a few missing ingots out of the next shipment.
You could take word to her that there'll be a shipment soon. Buy us some time and keep her off our backs.
In exchange, I'm willing to let a few ingots go missing. Maybe more than a few.
That's good news. I was starting to fear something had happened.
You have my thanks.
Well, no.
But that's not the point.
Bryling owns the mine, and she's not selling. We make her some decent coin running this place. Keeps her living well in Solitude.
At least, when we can get shipments to her. We're late on the last one. I expect her to start breathin' fire any moment now.
Is everything all right? I've not heard from Sorli or Pactur in some time.
I'm just protecting Bryling's investment. She's the owner, even though we're the ones here doing all the work.
She's already come down hard on us for missing shipments. Likely to do it again, since we're late.
No, and before you ask, it's not for sale.
Won't be 'some other time' at this rate.
They think he did it to get rid of his wife so he could be with Alva.
He moved in with her the day after the fire. Even I find that suspicious.
Lust can make a man do the unthinkable.
The ashes were still warm when he pledged himself to Alva.
Hey, greenskin... close your mouth. Some of us are trying to eat over here.
They say... well, they say he murdered someone. Something to do with Svidi.
You have a kind heart; much too kind for Riften. Don't worry about me, I'll be just fine.
Don't let it get you down. Indaryn will eventually tire of picking on you and move on to someone else.
Are you sure that's the real reason? It wouldn't have anything to do with others making an Argonian unwelcome, because if it is...
Svana, I heard that Sibbi was put in jail. What happened?
And do what? Losing a job from the Black-Briar family would make me a marked man. No one would ever take me on anywhere else.
The cold stone, the dampness... it suits me, Svana.
Maybe it's time you quit then.
Madesi, why don't you just take a bed here? There's no need to sleep in Beggar's Row.
I can't take it anymore, Tythis. Indaryn keeps pushing me around and I'm not even doing anything wrong.
If he wasn't a Black-Briar, I'd march right over to the jail and teach Sibbi some manners... but then I suppose I'd end up like Wulfur.
You're not going to goad me into a fight so I'll get kicked out, Grelka. Eat your food and leave me in peace.
Visiting Riften? All are welcome at the Temple of Mara.
Please to meet you, I'm a priestess at the Temple of Mara.
I really need your help! I don't want to lose my job at the fishery! What am I going to do?
Or perhaps you should crawl back into whatever swamp you came from, lizard man.
No, it was Svidi's brother, Wulfur. She's in hiding now. Somewhere far from here I'd suspect.
There's no need to be rude, Grelka. If you don't care for Argonians, then perhaps you should take residence somewhere else.
Come to think of it, I haven't seen Svidi in a while. Wait... he didn't...
Don't have to tell me twice. But then, I'm not the one that buried iron under these mountains.
I can go back to my life for now.
And I have you to thank for it. I won't forget this.
A risky game to play, friend.
But now Kematu's gone, and the rest of his snakes should slither back to their desert.
You could have warned me you were baiting me into a trap.
But you're right. This should scatter the rest of them. I can go back to my life for now.
You have my thanks.
You liar!
How could you do that to me!
Dragonborn. It is you. Welcome to High Hrothgar.
Come, we will do your next test inside the hall.
I am Master Arngeir. I speak for the Greybeards.
Now tell me, Dragonborn, why have you come here?
Well, you got Maven, she pretty much runs the whole operation. She's got friends in high places if you know what I mean.
She's also got ties to the Dark Brotherhood and the Thieves Guild, so basically no one can touch her.
Just remember, if she tells you to do somethin'... you damn well better do exactly what she says.
Yeah? Well, I got news for you; there's nothing to see here.
Last thing the Black-Briars need is some stranger stickin' their nose where it doesn't belong.
I don't know you. You in Riften lookin' for trouble?
Slip up and I'll gut you like a fish.
Be seein' you around.
Pleasure doin' business with you.
So what do you want to know?
You don't have enough to earn my loyalty.
Then we're speakin' the same language. Good.
So what do you want to know?
Then you're stupider than you look.
You better watch your ass around here.
You can pretend not to hear me all you want... but you better stay out of the Black-Briars' business.
Hey, you. Get over here, I want to talk to you.
Stay out of the Black-Briars' business and you'll live longer.
You aren't worth my time. Piss off.
I'm through with you. Get out of my way.
What did you want?
The Black-Briars have Riften in their pocket and the Thieves Guild watchin' their back, so keep your nose out of their business.
Me? I'm Maul. I watch the streets for 'em. If you need dirt on anythin', I'm your guy... but it'll cost you.
Yeah, walk away like a good doggie. Just stay out of the Black-Briars' business and maybe we'll give you a bone.
That's the wrong answer.
Last thing the Black-Briars need around here is some troublemaker tryin' to steal a piece of the action.
Suit yourself.
I'll be at the docks if you change your mind.
Don't say something you'll regret.
Last thing the Black-Briars need is some loudmouth tryin' to meddle in their affairs.
You kiddin'? My brother Dirge works in their hideout.
I used to run with them myself, but took a job with Maven after they started hittin' a rough patch.
If you want to get in on that action, find Brynjolf in the marketplace. I'm sure he could use someone like you.
All right, all right, let's not get too hasty now.
So what do you want to know?
This is a shame.
Always sad when someone has to die.
Another one. Terrible.
Good!
All right, let's give this a try. Ready your ward...
It doesn't matter if they're in the city. We fight them where they live, we won't have to fight them here.
Very well, you may approach the Mournful Throne, but watch your words.
It's not that simple, Igmund. There are reports of Forsworn here in the city.
You. Who are you to approach the Jarl of Markarth?
The only thing we need to discuss is how many men it'll take to flush them out of the hills.
My Jarl, we need to discuss the Forsworn.
I don't know you, and I don't sell what precious property we have to outsiders.
But if you're willing to work, and you prove to be reliable, I might deem you worthy to live here. My steward will handle things from there.
Yes. The finest court in all of High Rock. You think politics here are something? Well, we Bretons invented politics.
That is where I learned to cook, and where I belong. Not here, with all these dogs.
As you wish.
Nothing to say. Fine, but I'm watching you.
As you were, then.
I don't blame you.
My mother and father yell a lot. Always have.
My father is more generous than he lets on. Gives me plenty of spending money.
Just here to drink.
Drinks here are watered down, but they're cheap.
Treat my people fairly, and I will do the same to you.
They live here too, my Jarl. That's what I'm telling you.
You've honored my father's memory in more ways than I have words to say.
Do what you will. Cicero has no fight left. In the end, Sithis will judge us both.
You're back. Good. You'll want to hear this.
Do me a favor and kill that little jester twice. Just to make sure.
I don't, I'm afraid. Ranmir didn't talk much about her after she'd left.
He seemed convinced that she'd run off with someone named Vex. That's all I remember.
Like I told you, last time I saw her she was headed for Hobs Fall Cave. No idea what happened to her.
See, Ranmir was involved with a pretty young woman named Isabelle Rolaine. The two of them were very happy together, I thought.
One day, she just disappeared. Ranmir was convinced she'd run off with someone else, leaving him behind.
He turned to drinking, and what's left of him is what you see here every day.
By the Eight. She's dead, isn't she? And it's all my fault.
This... This is really from her, isn't it?
Well, I'm getting to that. Just let me explain.
Not him again.
He came nosing around Riften a while back, making a lot of noise about me. He was... persuaded to leave before he found me.
Funny, I'd have thought she would've gone back to him by now.
Huh?
Ha. Sure you did. Dagur put you up to this?
It don't matter now. None of it does. Just leave me be.
It's about Ranmir.
Oh, I'm so glad you changed your mind.
It's about Ranmir.
Did she really run off and do this? For me?
I... I have some thinking to do. Thank you for this.
Oh, he was a different person. Happy, outgoing... Like anyone who's in love.
Don't get me wrong, I don't have a problem with him being here all the time. He mostly keeps to himself, and so far he's been able to pay.
I just hate to see what's happened to him. He hasn't always been like this, you know. Drunk, I mean.
I'm not sure. I hoped that maybe if she could be found, if he had some sort of explanation, that maybe he could put himself back together.
I'm in no position to go searching for her, but perhaps you could.
A what?
Don't you go pulling my leg. Give it here.
Well, ain't gonna find her here. Haven't seen her in a while.
Well, not exactly.
Listen, we're friends, right?
I wondered if I could ask a favor of you.
Please, leave me alone. I need to be by myself.
After you talk to Brynjolf and Mercer, come back and see me.
Who's asking?
Ah. Well, some other time, perhaps.
Unless it's gold for another round of drinks, I don't wanna hear it.
She came through a while ago, wanted to know where she could get her hands on something valuable. Anything, really.
I told her it was a bad idea, that she wasn't cut out for what'd be necessary, but she wouldn't hear it.
So I gave her some advice, pointed her towards Hobs Fall Cave and that was that.
What?
Don't you... Don't you say that name to me.
Don't you dare. Ever.
For the moment we have ceased hostilities. We'll see what these Imperials have to say. I doubt we'll come to any lasting agreement.
Things are quiet for the moment. That won't last long. As soon as a new campaign starts, I want you there. Stay close. See me tomorrow.
Did you reconsider? Will you talk to my father?
Well, if you change your mind, I'd still like your help.
Lead the way!
Seemed like the best way to see the world and make some coin.
Sure I'm a little green, but what I lack in experience, I make up for in courage.
Can't blame you for having better things to do than help some farmer you don't know.
I can't thank you enough, friend. I'll hope you'll come back to Rorikstead soon and pay me a visit.
Maybe we can swap stories about our adventures over a mug of ale at the inn!
Are you new around here? You should go to the inn if you need a place to stay.
Sissel thinks she's so special. No way she's more special than me.
I had a dream that there was a good dragon. He was old and gray, but he wasn't scary.
Most days, I do all I can to stay away from my sister and my father. The beating's the same from either one.
I got enough troubles, so don't go adding to them.
Did you have any luck talking to my father?
I hope you can change my father's mind. He can be a little stubborn.
If you need a meal or a room, I've got both.
You talk to Reldith yet? She's like a mother to me.
Reldith is the hardest-working farmer in Rorikstead, but I'm the one with a head for business.
We're honest, hardworking folk here, and we don't suffer beggars or thieves.
City folk might look down on us, but what's wrong with farming? Everything we've got, we had to earn with our own sweat.
Sorry, I don't mean to be rude but Jouane's the one who deals with people. I'm afraid I lost my charm years ago.
If you're thinking of setting up to farm, you picked the right place.
I'm sure it's hard to believe, but I was once a bold adventurer like you, ready to face any danger.
Have you spoken to Erik? His father won't let him go out and seek his fortune in the world. Maybe you can help.
Good to see you again! How would you like to hire a brave, if untested, mercenary?
At first I thought I'd miss farming. I guess I've had my fill of it, because I don't miss it at all.
It just feels right somehow, having this new life as an adventurer. It's what I was meant to do.
This new armor fits me like a glove.
If I can make enough money here in Skyrim, I think I'll travel to Hammerfell and see what work I can get there.
Not yet. You know you're asking for some pretty strange stuff, don't you? It's not easy to find.
Always looking on the bright side, aren't you.
You assured me that you could find it, however.
I did, and I will. It's just tougher than I thought.
You looking for anything in particular this time?
I certainly hope so, otherwise I'm wasting my time.
No, without Ranmir around we're running out of stock far less often than we used to.
Don't sass me, little girl.
Perhaps, perhaps. I'm not quite sure what I need.
How are you today, Thaena?
Well, you know the drill. You don't see it here, it's gonna cost you extra for me to get it.
I'm fully aware of the terms of our... arrangement.
Yeah, well I don't see anything.
Things are easier now that I don't have to worry about Ranmir anymore.
I still can't believe you were able to help Ranmir pull himself together.
Without all our money going to drink, I can afford to expand my selection a bit.
Have you gotten the items I requested from you?
You got anything in here worth buying?
Same as ever. Trying to keep from freezing to death, trying to keep food on the table.
That's right. Welcome to Markarth, traveler. Thank the Divines you can choose to live somewhere else.
Be careful not to fall on your way out.
I have silver to bend.
Arnleif and Sons Trading Company. I'm a porter. Just some muscle hired to move heavy packages.
But we don't get any shipments in. Forsworn attack every caravan. So all I do is sit here and drink.
I hate giving speeches.
Not while I'm still breathing, it's not.
The Legion will be staying here for quite some time. I don't expect there to be any further violence.
Sovngarde... sir.
Ulfric Stormcloak!
You are guilty of insurrection, murder of Imperial citizens, the assassination of King Torygg, and high treason against the Empire.
It's over.
Brunwulf. Yes, I've sent men to protect him and bring him here.
Windhelm will need a government quickly if we are to prevent more violence.
Fine by me.
Well Ulfric, you can't escape from me this time.
Any last requests before I send you to... to wherever you people go when you die.
I am proud of all of you.
All hail the Emperor. All hail his Legionnaires!
Already done, sir.
Well, the men will be expecting some kind of speech.
And we'll need to hand the city over to that Free-Winter fellow.
Come, Rikke. There's still much to be done.
Well? What are we waiting for?
Secure the door.
Nothing.
Just saying goodbye.
We couldn't have done this without you. The Empire glories in your accomplishments...
Enough!
You are traitors and will die traitors' deaths. Stand down and face public execution, or advance and face summary execution by my hands.
It matters little to me. Either way I'll be sending your heads back to Cyrodiil.
In appreciation for your exemplary service, I am doubling your pay and compensation to the widows of your fallen comrades.
What was that, Legate?
I pray you're right, Legate.
In the meantime, we'll continue to root them out and put them to the sword.
Here, use my sword to do it. I made sure to sharpen it for the occasion.
No... But I belong to her.
We are turning the city over to Brunwulf Free-Winter, an honorable and faithful man.
Many of you will be staying in Windhelm to aid the Jarl in restoring order and stamping out any embers of rebellion that may still smolder here.
Song or not, I just want it done.
Talos be with you...
Skyrim doesn't belong to you, Ulfric.
His head will be sent to Cyrodiil where it will adorn a spike on the walls of the Imperial City.
Let this day be a final warning to all who would still call themselves Stormcloaks.
It'll make for a better song.
Oh, and Legate. It's been an honor to have you at my side. As a token of my appreciation, I want you to keep my sword.
And now, to deliver a speech.
There's bound to be resistance. There are many Stormcloak camps tucked away in the hills.
They'll no doubt strike whenever and wherever they can.
But without Ulfric to inflame their passions, they'll settle down and return to their homes eventually.
I'll never surrender Skyrim into the hands of a corrupt and dying Empire.
Let the Dragonborn be the one to do it.
I hope we haven't just created a martyr.
The rebellion is over. Ulfric Stormcloak is dead.
Well, I'm not quite done myself.
Right. Well?
It wasn't so bad.
Step aside Galmar. We're here to accept Ulfric's surrender.
Attention! General Tullius has an announcement!
My spies report the Jarl's Steward, Anuriel, has arrangements with the Thieves Guild that would be rather embarrassing if made public.
I need you to find evidence of her corrupt activities, and use it to gain a measure of her "cooperation."
Rumor has it the Jarl's Steward, Raerek, is a faithful Talos worshipper, if not a true son of Skyrim - he still supports the Empire, after all.
But, if confronted with proof of his belief, you might be able to "persuade" him to aid our cause, indirectly of course.
Wouldn't want to sully his reputation, would we?
I told you everything I know. What else did you want?
You looking for something?
I thank you for your help, my friend.
I should be the one thanking you. You've solved more problems in the short time you've been here then anyone in my court has their entire careers.
You've been a beacon of hope in these dark times for my people and I will never forget it.
Therefore, by my authority as Jarl, I pronounce you Thane of Riften and award you all of the benefits befitting your station. Congratulations.
I expect that you'll distance yourself from the affairs of state. In other words, keep your nose out of my business and we'll do just fine.
Just remember that you're only a part of my court, this doesn't make you one of the family.
I guess all that's left is to bestow the title. What were those words again?
Doesn't matter... the title is yours. I henceforth name you Thane of Riften. Don't disappoint me.
Well done! Your continued efforts have been of great benefit to the people of Riften.
Allow me to present you with this compensation for your selfless efforts.
I'll send my men to secure any of the remaining goods you left behind. We can't have that substance falling into the wrong hands now, can we?
Here, this should suffice as your cut of the... or rather, a reward for your actions. Now, if you'll excuse me...
Much like Mjoll, you've become champion of our hold; helping people with their difficulties and providing assistance for their needs.
As the Jarl of Riften, I feel it's my duty to honor your selfless behavior by honoring you with the title Thane of Riften.
Oh don't pretend to be modest. I'm talking about your new role as champion. Apparently, you've taken my people under your wing.
If you think you can steal my throne by winning the hearts of the people, that's where you're wrong. Keep that in mind.
However, since it wouldn't behoove me to make you disappear, I'd rather have you on my side as Thane of Riften.
Ah, there you are. I was wondering when you'd visit us again.
I've been informed by my steward that you've made quite an impression in the Rift; several of our citizens have expressed their admiration.
So, I've been hearing your name in court quite a bit lately.
I've been informed by my steward that you've made quite an impression in the Rift; the people won't stop blathering about you.
You've already taken all the necessary steps in becoming eligible for the title.
Therefore all that remains is to bestow it upon you.
You damn well better.
I've checked with my court and apparently you're fully eligible... there's nothing standing in the way of presenting you the title.
Before I can present you with the title, there's but a single requirement you must fulfill.
A Thane of Riften is expected to maintain permanent residence within the city walls.
My steward has such a home available to you for purchase. I think you'll find the accommodations to be most pleasing.
When the house is yours, return to me and I will present you the title.
Not so fast, there's a matter of residence to be discussed.
In order to have the title bestowed upon you, you need to purchase a house within Riften.
And by the Eight, aren't you fortunate that my steward has just the place for you to buy.
After the place is yours, come back to me and we'll deal with the rest of the nonsense.
I think I've said enough. Now leave me alone.
Be careful, Niluva is a bit... aggressive when she's cornered.
Then allow me to officially welcome you to Riften. You'll make a fine addition to our city.
All that remains now is to bestow the title upon you.
Hmm? What's this about?
Oh, yes. Of course... the whole thane thing. I'd forgotten. If you're ready then, can we get this out of the way?
Suit yourself. I'm always here.
Here we go!
Not at all. It's good to be put in your place once in a while.
I guess I shouldn't boast so much... it gets expensive!
Sure, I don't mind taking some more of your gold.
Just let me know when you're ready.
I'll fight you again, but not until you have the gold to back up your bet.
I don't blame you. If I were in your shoes, I'd chicken out too.
Well, offer still stands if you ever want to have a go.
I don't believe it. I guess you were right!
All right, remember... this is a clean fight. No weapons, no tricks. And none of those fancy magic spells, either.
Here we go!
Well come back when you earn some more so I can take it from you!
You up for another wager?
Back, eh? Ready to brawl?
Got horses that need shoeing, so make it quick.
Oh! Sounds like I've got a challenger.
Tell you what. How about a friendly wager, just between us. I bet you my entire purse of septims I can beat you in a fistfight.
You match the bet and we fight until one of us gives in.
Bah, I'm not afraid of anything out here. Walls are just a weakling's hiding spot.
Now me, I'm no weakling. In fact, I bet I've got the strongest arms in all of Skyrim.
Forgive me milord, I didn't recognize you! Please proceed.
Forgive me milady, I didn't recognize you! Please proceed.
Bloody tourists.
Sure, that'll do just fine. Go on ahead.
That's just insulting. Not even a proper bribe?
Oh, my mistake then. Sorry, you can go on ahead.
Sure, and I'm Queen Barenziah. Now tell me another one.
Hey, you're not supposed to be down here. Official business only.
Is it daylight out? Night? I lose track down here.
The prisoners are in rare form, as usual.
Watch your step in here. The stonework gets slippery.
Now that the Empire's arrived in Riften, we've finally established a launching point into Morrowind... just in case.
I'm kind of busy, but if you need any potions or ingredients, let me know.
I'll be over here if you need any potions or ingredients.
Hello there, can I pour you a Black-Briar Mead?
Ah good, back for some more Black-Briar mead, eh?
Welcome to the Pawned Prawn. Come on in, take a look around.
Welcome back to the Prawn. Let me know if you need anything.
What are you doing down here?
Ignore me all you want, you still aren't getting in.
I trust the day's found you well, thane.
How do you do, thane?
The fiercest of the remaining rebels will continue to harass us, but by in large, the people here desire peace.
What I'm not so sure about is the peace we've made with the Thalmor. But we'll keep that between the two of us, alright?
There are those who still call themselves Stormcloaks, who continue to fight us, and bring misery to the people... but they are few in number.
The ordinary citizen will be happy to get back to life as normal, to have their families return home.
And they should enjoy it while they can... I suspect all of Tamriel will again be called to arms in the not too distant future.
There will be peace for a time, during which we must rebuild Skyrim into the land it once was. Strong. Self-reliant. The center of mankind.
Because getting rid of the Empire was only half the problem.
Soon, the elves will again seek to rule the world. We must ready ourselves to fight them.
For it will be Skyrim that shall lead Tamriel in those dark days, when the fate of the world is finally determined.
Peace? When has any Nord ever known peace? Skyrim breeds warriors. It's who we are, it's in our blood.
And that's as it should be. In the darkest days ahead, it'll be us Nords who lead the fight against the elves.
It's only a matter of time before their pointy ears start itching to rule the world again.
It'll be Skyrim that leads the charge. Mark my words. And that time around, mankind will either succeed once and for all or die trying.
Ulfric is dead and his army destroyed. There's not much more I could ask of you. The gods, however, I suspect, may have plans for you elsewhere.
