Listen, could you take the sword to my father, Proventus Avenicci? He's the Jarl's steward. He'll know the right time to present it to him.
None may pass this perilous bridge 'til I judge them worthy by the warrior's test.
Goodness, no!
They're simply scholars, they mean no one harm. I've had... several conversations with Arch-Mage Aren over the years.
He's perfectly polite, if a bit guarded.
Just about eighty years ago, there was a terrible disaster. The cliffs overlooking the Sea of Ghosts collapsed, taking most of Winterhold with them.
In the middle of it all, the College was practically untouched. Many of the survivors were suspicious.
Some believed the mages were behind the whole thing, and others felt they could have at least prevented it.
Arch-Mage Aren assures me that his people had nothing to do with it, and I believe him. But Winterhold never recovered.
You may have noticed the College, just to the north there. Very prestigious place. Mages from all over Tamriel traveled here to seek knowledge.
After the Oblivion Crisis was over, many felt that magic users were to blame. Elves, to be specific. It created a great deal of tension.
A good many dark elves were driven from the city, and people became... uncomfortable with the presence of the College.
Yeah, but raising Draugr to take on both the Imperials and the Stormcloaks? That's just crazy.
I think it's sweet Lu'ah is so worked up over her dead soldier...
True, but I'm not going to be the one to try to oppose her...
Shor's Bones! Look at that monstrosity.
This is where we saw Mercer. We must be getting close.
I can hear water rushing through these pipes. We must be beneath a lake.
Aye, lass... and we'll be ready for him.
Let's keep moving.
Looks like we can take the low road or the high road across this chamber. Your choice.
Aye. Whatever you want to do, we're with you.
The stench... this place reeks of Falmer.
It's a Dwarven Centurion. Very tough and very deadly.
It's Mercer! Look... down there!
So this is what we heard. The entire tower collapsed.
Wait a moment... what's that?
Falmer patrol up ahead. I don't think they know we're here.
Look at the size of this place. Have you ever seen anything like it in your life, lass?
Brynjolf and I found them like that. Mercer's doing.
We have to catch up to him before it's too late.
This must be their hive. We'll have to keep silent if we want to avoid drawing their attention.
The only reason to do that would be to block pursuit. It must be Mercer.
We'll have to find another way around.
Damn it! There's no way through.
Mercer must have triggered these pistons. We're going to have to search for a way to open them.
He's close. I'm certain of it. We must prepare ourselves.
I'm on it, lass.
Even the Falmer don't deserve the pain these implements must have inflicted. The dwarves were a cruel race.
I'll bet my last septim that there's a mechanism around here somewhere.
He's toying with us. He wants us to follow.
Then this is it. We do this for Gallus and for the Guild.
We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lad. We're right behind you.
We can take the beast on or sneak around. It's your call, lass. We're right behind you.
Can't say that I have. Imagine the riches hidden within these walls.
There's a mass of the Falmer in this chamber. We can sneak through or take them down... I don't care. As long as we get to Mercer.
It's the past! Dead oaths on dead lips. Let it stay there.
Your ancestors wouldn't want this, Silus!
The museum is opening, Madena.
Why should I hide from it? This is my family's legacy!
There's a new museum opening up in Dawnstar. The owner is asking me to hand out invitations to travelers.
My Amulet of Arkay. It's the source of my divine powers and also a sacred badge of office.
I misplaced it in the catacombs. I'd look for it, but I've been hearing noises down there. I fear the dead have become... restless.
Without that amulet, I'm powerless to confront them. Would you be willing to search for it?
Ah, splendid, splendid. It's for a special brew I'm working on. A love elixir like none other. Maybe I'll test it on Farengar first...
Oh, but I suppose you expect some compensation. Um... here, these potions should suffice.
I'll make sure the Emperor himself hears of all you've accomplished. You've made Skyrim a better place.
The leader of the Dark Brotherhood? You mean Astrid? Dead? And this is no jest?
Ha! This is a stroke of good fortune. Long have I watched the Dark Brotherhood's movements... waiting for the time to strike. That time is now!
My agents have recently acquired the passphrase to their Sanctuary. It is, "Silence, my brother." Every assassin in that hole must be put down!
You, my friend. You've slain their leader. This honor should be yours. Do this, and you will be rewarded most handsomely!
We have a chance to destroy the Dark Brotherhood, forever. It's all up to you.
By all I hold holy... Can it be true? Have you actually done it?
This is a great day, my friend. You have struck a blow against darkness that will not soon be forgotten! Here, as promised - a most generous reward!
You did what?
By the gods, you're serious. You'd better report this to Commander Maro right away. He's at the Penitus Oculatus outpost, up in Dragon Bridge.
I believe I am, yes. Technically speaking, of course. The city is also home to a priest, priestess, an alchemist, and I'm sure others who practice.
Ah, that reminds me. Speaking of alchemists, I have some frost salts for Arcadia. She asked me to obtain them for one of her potions.
Would you be so kind as to deliver the frost salts for me? I'm sure Arcadia will provide some form of recompense...
The Jarl has put out a bounty on some bandits. Here, take a look at this decree for details.
Mercer was able to knock this thing down? Gods...
It's the Key, Brynjolf. In his hands, there's no telling what he's capable of.
Not this again.
But I'm thirsty now.
Not now. If I don't pull night duty, I'll drink a round with you at the Sleeping Giant.
What? What is it now, mother?
If he does, tell him how bad the food is. And how dangerous it is.
It was as big as the mountain, and black as night. It flew right over the barrow.
Look old woman. If your boy wants to learn how to fight, we'll teach him. Now off with you.
You're always thirsty. Just like you're always drunk. Now let me get back to my rounds.
A dragon! I saw a dragon!
Has my son Sven asked to join the war?
My favorite drinking buddy! Let's get some mead.
Dragons, now, is it? Please, mother. If you keep on like this everyone in town will think you're crazy.
Has the Bloodmoon called you, fellow Hunter?
The prey is strong. Stronger than the hunters.
But more will come. Bring him down, for the glories of Lord Hircine.
Can it be? You've brought me my amulet? Oh, thank Arkay. And thank you. Oh, dear friend, please, take this gold for your troubles.
What the... we've been betrayed! Look out!
No. I will have no peace until these hunters are dead.
There he is! Get him!
This is the resting place of Ysgramor. And his most trusted generals. You should be cautious.
For Kodlak!
Don't get too flowery on me, lass.
Oh it's you. The one from the market.
No.... Not again!
No more.... No more!
Don't kill me.... I don't want to die!
I... won't... surrender!
Call... that... a... swing?
You'll pay for this....
I'll never submit!
Do your worst, monster!
I won't bend. Never!
We drink to our youth, to days come and gone. For the age of aggression is just about done.
We'll drive out the Stormcloaks and restore what we own. With our blood and our steel we'll take back our home.
Down with Ulfric the killer of kings. On the day of your death we'll drink and we'll sing.
J'zargo is eager to hear how the scrolls are working.
J'zargo has seen that Tolfdir likes you.
You must be very skilled with magic, yes?
J'zargo is working hard, but cannot do it alone. Can you help with this?
Brynjolf's been sending idiots like you down here for years looking for their hideout.
Funny thing is his stupid Thieves Guild never counted on me and my partner blocking the way.
Now, empty your pockets or I'll pick the gold off your corpse.
Hey, you. Stop right there!
Empty your pockets or end up as skeever food.
I'm going to check the entrance to the Ratway. Be right back.
Okay, okay.
Don't worry, Hewnon will let you pass.
We won't give you any more trouble.
Sorry to have bothered you. I'll just be out of your way now.
What are you lookin' at?
Ignoring me, huh? Suit yourself. Get 'em Hewnon!
You worry about bashing people's heads in, I'll worry about the Guild. Okay?
Suit yourself. Get 'em Hewnon!
This far? We're livin' in a sewer. You said we'd have a house as big as the Black-Briars' by now.
You aren't going to pass. You're going to give me everything you have. Whether you leave here in one piece is up to you.
Why are you always acting like such a big baby? I've gotten us this far.
I dunno, Drahff. They'd skin us alive if they knew we were doin' this.
No need to get hasty. I... I was just testing you. You can go on ahead.
Let 'em go, Hewnon. All clear.
I doubt that very much.
Let's finish this.
Treachery! Attack!
J'zargo has worked hard on learning new spells, trying new things. This will make J'zargo stand out as a mage.
But J'zargo works so hard, there is no time to test these new spells. If you help with the testing, this will make learning easier.
J'zargo will give you scrolls, you will use them and report the results. This is a good plan, no?
You tell J'zargo when you are ready.
Agh! Pathetic mortal!
You should buy some produce from Carlotta's stall. Most of it comes from my farm.
You should stop by my farm if you get a chance. See what life's really about.
There's a nip in the air. Cold won't be good for my crops. Never is.
I own the Pelagia Farm, just outside the city walls. Nimriel takes good care of things for me.
I had my years of war and fighting. Senseless, all of it. All I want now is a rake and a patch of good soil.
Close...
You'll need to do better than that.
Easily blocked!
Oho! Not so fast!
...gonna start keepin' a knife in my boot... tired of gettin' disarmed...
...should have known she was lying... said she'd wait for me... but they never wait...
...lyin' little harlot... that brat ain't mine... could be anyone's... won't get one rusty septim from me...
..."go to the college" da said... "use your smarts" he said... like I'm supposed to figure out which college he meant...
...hmph, wizards... Now that's power... Bet they got that "secret" magic... can turn wood to gold... yeah... Wish I could turn wood to gold...
...mead, mead, mead... ...kill 'em to get some beer every now and then? ...stupid bees and their stupid honey...
...picking pockets, now that's the real art... ...no rough stuff, just take it and go... ...hmph, don't sound like much fun, though...
...once was a woman, as fair as an evenin', of springtime in old Stros M'Kai...
...with three beers down, the Orc did frown, and bid the Elf goodbye... ...for none could know, 'twas not for show, and someone had to die...
...kill him, he talks to me like that again... ...get him while he's sleeping... ...or poison his meat... ...see how he likes that...
I swear to you, unnatural magics are coming from that cave! There are strange noises and lights! We need someone to investigate!
Your eminence, my scrying has suggested nothing in the area. Dragon Bridge is under imperial control. This is likely superstitious nonsense.
Then we will immediately send out a legion to scour the cave and secure the town. Haafingar's people will always be safe under my rule.
Die already so I can take your stuff!
Whatcha gonna do? Huh? Whatcha gonna do?
Gonna split your belly like an old woman's purse!
Gonna rip you... open!
Hrraaggh!
Nyyarrggh!
Yeeeaaagghh!
Yeagh!
Rragh!
Hyunh!
Arrggh!
Nord... dog!
Filthy... barbarian!
You are no true Elf!
Fall to the Thalmor!
Bow to your betters!
On your knees, worm!
Pathetic human!
Suffering to all mankind!
Disgusting... beast!
Fear the Thalmor!
My thirst will be sated...
Oh, I can smell your blood...
Tell you what. You start running so I can stab you in the back.
Can't wait to count out your coin...
Fire, frost, or lightning - you will suffer at my hands!
Don't you see? I am master of the arcane!
First you... then all of the Reach!
The Forsworn will reclaim what is rightfully ours!
Foolish human. Your time is at its end!
Time for you humans to learn your place!
Soon, all Nords will be thralls to the Thalmor!
Nord beast! Both your life and lands are now forfeit!
Behold, the future. Behold, the Thalmor!
Ha ha ha ha ha! You wish to defeat me? Impossible.
Don't you see? Elven supremacy is the only truth!
Death is the only way out of your misery!
You are but a dog, and I am your master!
Ha! You can't resist, can you?
Let's see... this one, I think. J'zargo has designed it to be an excellent flame cloak, with a twist.
It is especially potent against the undead, and should burn them to a crisp in no time.
J'zargo gives you plenty of these scrolls, so you try them and return when you can say whether they work.
We were betrayed by one of our own kind. In fact, I'm to blame for what's happened here.
I haven't heard that name in a long time.
How do you know of me?
I was blinded. Blinded by dark treachery masquerading as friendship.
Perhaps if I had been more vigilant, then Mercer Frey wouldn't have lured me to my fate and stolen the Skeleton Key.
I'm afraid so. I'm weakening, and I can feel myself slipping away. The years without restoration of my power have taken their toll.
Whatever damage has been caused can only be corrected by following the Pilgrim's Path to the Ebonmere and replacing the Key.
I don't recognize you, but I sense that you're one of us. Who are you?
The Sepulcher isn't merely a temple or a vault to house the Key. Within these walls is the Ebonmere... a conduit to Nocturnal's realm of Evergloam.
When Mercer stole the Key, that conduit closed, severely limiting our ties to her.
With the Ebonmere closed, and their sudden severance from the realm of Evergloam, I fear they've undergone a drastic change.
They're shadows of their former selves. They no longer remember their true purpose or their original identities.
Nothing would bring me more pride than to return the Key, but I'm afraid it's impossible.
From the moment I arrived here, I've felt myself... well... dying.
My spirit didn't manifest itself in the Sepulcher immediately, so fortunately I wasn't present when the Ebonmere was sealed.
However, ever since that day, I've felt my power waning... slowly draining away.
The last of the Nightingale Sentinels, I'm afraid. I've defended the Sepulcher alone for what seems like an eternity.
You'll serve me soon enough.
You've arrived at last. The hero who prevented me from being bound returns to my fold.
You've arrived at last. The heroine who prevented me from being bound returns to my fold.
I have much to thank you for, little one. When you die I will raise you and you can take your place by my side.
You've done the Guild a great deed. And although they may not show it, I'm certain they appreciate your sacrifices.
My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
Were I able to provide it, I'd shower you in wealth for what you've done.
My only regret is that you had to undertake this task alone.
Yes, absolutely. Your skill is your own... don't let anyone ever tell you otherwise.
But Nocturnal, she influences our luck; nearly imperceptible assistance we get when we ply our trade.
Precisely. There are a few who still call Nocturnal "Lady Luck" and for good reason.
Think about the Guild... about the state it was in when you began. Think about all the little things you might have heard.
A pick breaking when it shouldn't have, the clouds in the nighttime sky clearing at the wrong moment...
Our access to those bits of luck are what separates us from common bandits.
That burned paper is all that remains of the fabled Mysterium Xarxes. The blasphemous book written by Mehrunes Dagon himself.
It's said that Mankar Camoran used the book to open a portal to a Paradise where all his followers would live forever.
Feel free to look around. Come talk to me when you're ready to discuss that job I mentioned.
The Commentaries on the Mysterium Xarxes were written by the Mythic Dawn cult leader, Mankar Camoran.
He promised a paradise awaited his followers when they died, that they would be reborn by Mehrunes Dagon's side.
The tapestries hung here and outside were found in hideouts where members of the Mythic Dawn would meet and plot.
The cult's greatest accomplishment was the assassination of the Septim Dynasty and the opening of the Oblivion Gates.
Those robes were worn during the Mythic Dawn's secret meetings, where they plotted to bring the Daedra Mehrunes Dagon into Tamriel.
Each bolt of yarn used to make the robe was colored with a dye made from sacrificial blood.
Did you have any questions about the museum, or would you rather talk business?
At long last! Alduin's doom is now ours to seal - just speak the word and with high hearts we'll hasten forth to smite the worm wherever he lurks.
Hold, comrades - let us counsel take before battle is blindly joined.
Speak with the Nervous Patron at Candlehearth Hall, in Windhelm. Accept his gold, then kill the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Speak with the Wary Outlaw at the Bee and Barb, in Riften. Accept his gold, and then eliminate the target. So begins a contract, bound in blood.
Yet another child has prayed to their mother.
If anybody asks, this meeting never took place.
I don't know you.
But our work is just beginning. Approach me, Listener, and hear my words.
Give those pirates what for!
What're you looking at?
And here you are. I hope you've found the museum to your liking.
Our master craftsman are nothing compared to the perfection of the Daedra. Look at how it shines.
Here's your gold, as promised.
A little history, first. After the Oblivion Crisis, a number of groups cropped up dedicated to wiping out the remnants of the Mythic Dawn.
One of these groups found Mehrunes' Razor, the artifact of Dagon. They split it into three fragments and pledged to keep them apart forever.
That was almost 150 years ago, and the pieces are still being kept by the descendants of that group. And they're right here, in Skyrim.
Did you try the grip? Isn't it eerie how it seems to mold itself into your hand?
Here's your gold.
Don't know what you're talking about, stranger.
At least two of the owners, Ghunzul and Drascua, are dangerous marauders. And the third owner, Jorgen, I only know he lives in Morthal.
Here are my notes about them. I'll gladly pay you for getting the pieces any way you can. No questions asked.
Look at them. The legendary sharpness of a deadric weapon. Marvelous.
And here's your payment for the shards.
Finally, all the pieces of Mehrunes' Razor are in my hands. It's time I let you in on something.
There's a fourth piece. That scabbard in the display case. Built to house the Razor. And there's more.
I know how to put all the pieces together. We just need to take them to Dagon's shrine and contact the Lord of Change directly.
Good luck finding the fragments.
I'll just be tending the museum if you need me.
What is it? Do you have one of the Razor fragments?
All right. Head to the pit when you think you're ready.
What is this, you think you're clever?
If you want to fight your way out of here, you have to do it in the Pit.
Gotta give the crowd a show.
Luckily there's no shortage of Pit bait around here. Now get in there and earn your freedom.
Why, perhaps the greatest crime spree Skyrim's ever known.
There are pockets brimming with coin and coffers overflowing with riches ripe for the picking.
We may be Nightingales, but in our hearts we're still thieves and we're damn good at what we do.
I wouldn't take that to heart. It's her way.
Think of her as a scolding mother continually pushing you harder to be successful; outwardly sounding angry but silently content.
I assure you, had she been displeased with you, we wouldn't be having this conversation.
This is Nocturnal's way of maintaining balance.
If you ever feel the need to change your abilities, you can return to the Sepulcher and step onto a different circle.
Be warned that once you've chosen, you can't reselect for at least a day.
The circles at the base of the Ebonmere imbue you with powers befitting a Nightingale Agent.
The crescent moon represents the Agent of Shadow, the half moon for the Agent of Subterfuge and the full moon for the Agent of Strife.
The Guild has welcomed me back with open arms. I feel like a void in my life has finally been filled.
I only hope that this isn't an ending to things, but actually the beginning.
Now, your life as a Nightingale begins. Should the need arise, you'll be summoned to the Sepulcher in order to defend it.
I'm glad you were able to bring the Key back safely.
Nocturnal seemed quite pleased with your efforts.
Yeah. I'm glad we can finally stand down- getting tired of looking over my shoulder all the time.
Well, it's all over now. Lieutenant said you could see the smoke from Whiterun.
I think we'll all feel better once we're back out to sea. Won't be long now.
Dragons, murder, rebellion... and we sail right into the middle of it with the Emperor on board.
Gods, I'll be glad to see the last of Skyrim.
Arniel, stay on your guard. We can't let harm come to these people.
Please remain calm!
Everyone please, stand back!
Is he dead?
What's happened? He's not...
You'd better get out of that Stormcloak gear. Today's not the day to go around dressed like a rebel.
Only Dagon can declare if a pawn is worth keeping. I. Have. Spoken.
They were worshippers of Mehrunes Dagon, the Daedric Lord of Destruction and Change.
The Mythic Dawn killed Uriel Septim VII and his heirs, triggering the events that lead to the Oblivion Crisis, when the Daedra invaded Tamriel.
All that remains of the infamous cult, I've gathered in my museum.
I am pleased, mortal. I will give you my Razor. Use it to wreak havoc on Tamriel.
You. Mortal. You are worthy of speaking to. You have claimed the pieces of my Razor. It has been an amusing game to witness.
But Dagon does not declare a winner while there is a pawn on the board. Kill Silus. He and his family have served their purpose.
Kill him. Take your rightful place as my champion, or I will crush you.
I planned to kill Alain myself, you know. Nilsine, too. But lost my nerve. I even brewed a special poison, Lotus Extract. Maybe you could use it?
Just coat your weapon with it. Then... you get the idea.
Spare me your pitiful pride. You are but a tool of my ambitions, mortal. Never forget that.
Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
You have been a worthy tool. Fill the world with destruction in my name.
Place your hands on my altar one last time, and you will witness the power of Mehrunes Dagon for yourself.
Your guy's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens and paying us good coin for nobody to know about it. Well, until now that is.
Tell you what. Go ahead and deal with your business for now. When you're done, find me and we'll deal with mine.
Aye, you've got a point there.
Passing on a golden opportunity is worse.
I may fall to your malice, but your fate is already written.
You may strike me down, but your doom is near.
You cannot defeat us! The Dragonborn still stands!
I've waited an eternity for this day.
My debt is repaid at last.
I vowed to have your head.
Alduin escaped us long ago. Today we take our well-tempered revenge.
For a hundred lifetimes my heart has burned for revenge on Alduin too long delayed.
Your doom has come, damned worm!
Nowhere to hide! Hunter becomes our prey!
Die, World-Eater, in despair and fear!
Use Dragonrend, to drag him down!
Down with you, damned worm!
For Skyrim! For Shor! For Sovngarde's freedom!
I've waited long for such a worthy opponent.
The joy of battle burns in your heart!
The path is closed, till your courage is tested.
Prove worthy by warrior's test, and cross the bridge to the blessed hall.
Stand together and we shall defeat him!
There he is!
No escape this time, foul worm!
Don't blame me if your feet get sore.
Then stop wastin' my time.
Didn't think you had the coin for it anyway.
Then off with ya. I've got better things to do.
Should have known you'd be a cheap one.
Then what are you wasting my time for?
Suppose so. Got one all saddled if you've got the coin.
If I had one. Sold my last one to you a bit ago.
Reckon you can. How much you willing to pay?
Nope. Sold my last one to some stranger.
It's for sale, if you've got the coin.
Not right now. You bought my last one.
That one with the saddle? How much are you offering?
Can't help you. They're all spoken for.
Don't mind if you do. What's your price?
If I had one you could. They're all spoken for.
I've got one all saddled up for you! What do you figure she's worth?
Not today. You should have come by earlier. Wait, didn't I sell me last one to you?
Sounds fair. She's yours. Like I said, she's all saddled.
When you got that much, we'll talk.
Fair enough. You can have her. She's the one with the saddle.
Didn't think you had the coin.
That's your best offer? I suppose so. She's the one with the saddle.
If you had it, you'd be riding and we wouldn't be talking.
That much? I mean, of course. If that's all you've got.
I'd sell her for that, but you ain't got it.
Sold. She's all yours. She's already saddled up.
That would work, except you ain't got.
Wish everyone haggled like you. She's all yours.
Too bad you don't have that much. Guess you'll have to walk.
Everyone on me. Let's show these Imperial milk drinkers what true Nords look like!
Rrrrraaaaaarrrrr!!!!!
Whiterun's walls are tall, but they are old and crumbling, like the Empire whose Legion lines them.
They'll have set barricades to slow us down.
Destroy them quickly, and take out the archers in the towers along the way.
The faster we take them out, the faster we can claim this city as ours!
This is it men!
Well, I'd better get back to work. You two make yourselves at home.
Well, I'd better get some sleep. Don't mind me, though. You two make yourselves at home.
Of course. Nothing gets done without my approval in this city. I have the Jarl's ear, and the guards in my pocket.
Anyone makes trouble for me and I pay a visit to the Thieves Guild. Make me angry and I contact the Dark Brotherhood.
You'd do well to remember that the next time you make such a stupid observation.
The Black-Briar family has always been allied with the Guild. Our connections with the Empire and within Skyrim make for a perfect fit.
I dare say the Guild owes its survival as much to my family as it does to its own people.
And you think that I care? Interesting.
I don't believe in getting my hands dirty. You have people that handle those sort of things... use them.
Mara's domain encompasses the emotions we strive the most to embrace; love, compassion and understanding.
It's difficult to appreciate her gifts in these dark times, but you should consider her light a beacon in the storm.
The question is, what isn't wrong with Riften.
This city is corrupt. Rotten to the core. No one cares about anything except themselves and how much coin they can make off the misery of others.
This heap of matchsticks is what everyone calls Haelga's Bunkhouse. Can you stay here? No.
The Bunkhouse is for the working man, not some sort of a luxury inn for tourists.
Of course not. You expect me to take care of it by myself?
My niece Svana helps me with the chores. She'd be more help if she kept her head out of the clouds.
She's been with me ever since her parents were killed by bandits and she was dropped in my lap.
Authorities? Have you been listening to what I said? They're all dirty. Every one of them.
The only way to get things done in this city is to keep your head down and pay off the right people.
The Forsworn have taken Kolskeggr Mine. We're the only ones left.
Troll's blood, what happened to you Pavo?
Gods.... You two head inside and get some rest. I'll send word to the Jarl.
I'm in charge of Left Hand Mine. You cause trouble, you answer to me.
You've come far, mortal, but can you stand against my inner council?  Let's see!
Don't applaud yourself too soon, worm!
Rip the eyes from her head!
Rip the eyes from his head!
Suit yourself.
Oh ho. Now that's the Dark Brotherhood I know and love. This for business or pleasure? Ah, what's it matter, right? I'll see that it gets done.
Yeah, we can do that. I'm thinking a sewer grate on the outskirts of Dawnstar. Leads to a tunnel, then inside. You'll be in or out, quick as a coney.
You're a little light on coin there, my friend. No gold, no refit.
Tell me what you need, and I'll get my people get on it.
I'm afraid that's it, my friend. I've done all I can. I hope the place is at least livable now. Good luck with, your, um... murders.
Banners, huh? Trying to pretty up the place? All right, you got it.
Now, might I offer some advice? You should go to Riften and find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before?
Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, hmm?
You do that, and I'll see what I can do about recruiting some new additions to our Family...
That'll come in handy, yeah? An alchemy station, maybe a little garden with some choice plants. All right, consider it done.
Heh. Chamber fit for a king and all that, right? No problem. Give ya a big bed, decorate the room real nice, maybe even throw in a few extra baubles.
Do not fail Kodlak. He's earned his place here, and does not deserve to fall prey to Alduin's insatiable hunger.
Hail, Dragonborn. That honor is also mine - to our shared birthright you'll bring new glory!
Dragonborn! Have you come to cleanse Sovngarde at long last of Alduin's foul mist?
Why does Shor forbid us to face Alduin? I fear no worm, World-Eater though he be.
I long to fight that foul worm, but by Shor's command we curb our wrath.
The mist is gone! A glorious day!
The World-Eater is dead! The way is now clear!
You saved us all. We will sing of you forever.
You'd better speak to the Jarl, then.
Oh, yes? We'll get to that, after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
Oh, yes? I wonder what that old busybody wants now.
Whatever it is, it will have to wait until after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
And I'm sure he told you it was urgent. Well, he's not the only Jarl in Skyrim with problems to deal with.
Whatever it is, it will have to wait until after I've finished dealing with this dragon situation.
So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years.
You'd better come in and tell me how you found me and what you want.
Delphine? How do you ... ? This is just a trick to get me to open the door, eh? I told you to go away!
You play so well.
A hard truth, that is. Let's drink to the fallen ones.
Stay away from Camilla.
Oh, Sven. You do know how to turn a girl's head.
You need to choose between those two boys. It ain't fair to Sven or Faendal to keep tuggin' on their heart strings like that.
Just stay away from her, or...or else.
It's bad enough my brother is harping on me to pick one. Don't you start too.
Ah, Camilla. Fair as the morning sun. But I ask you, how could she prefer your brooding to my romantic tunes?
Ay, we can get that for ya. We just finished the timber for those catapults.
Gimme a mug of ale.
A pint of your finest mead.
Some wine, please?
I'll have some mead.
Pour me an ale.
Coming right up.
Certainly.
With you as my muse, how could I not?
I'm just sayin' you should pick one to settle down with.
I got a new order in for shields. I'm gonna need some hardwood planks. None of that soft pine this time.
War may be good for business, but it's hard on families.
Go away! Leave me alone!
Hey, haven't I seen you around the Flagon? I never expected to see another Guild brother down here.
They grabbed me in the Ratway. The nerve of these bastards... they need to be taught a lesson.
They grabbed me in Riften. They seem to think I know something. They just kept asking me the same damn questions.
Would you two shut up before Brurid hears you messing around.
Funny coming from a woman who's breath smells like she just kissed a draugr.
And it shows, I've seen horkers with smaller guts.
It's been forever since I've eaten so well.
Blood on the wind today. Good hunt ahead of us.
You look strong. Come to Jorrvaskr and be a Companion!
I swear, every wanderer who shows up in Whiterun looks weaker than the last.
Fight with us and you'll have stories to tell. Come up to Jorrvaskr if you're worth anything in a fight.
You. Over here!
So we're just supposed to sit here while Arvel runs off with that golden claw?
I know I ran ahead with the claw, but I need help!
Is... is someone coming? Is that you Harknir? Bjorn? Soling?
Just shut it and keep an eye out for trouble.
Sweet breath of Arkay, thank you.
That dark elf wants to go on ahead, let him. Better than us risking our necks.
What if Arvel doesn't come back? I want my share from that claw!
No. Not again. Help!
Where are you going? Come back!
Does it look like I can move? You have to cut me down, first.
You fool, why should I share the treasure with anyone?
Yes, the claw. I know how it works. The claw, the markings, the door in the Hall of Stories. I know how they all fit together!
Help me down, and I'll show you. You won't believe the power the Nords have hidden there.
It's coming loose. I can feel it.
You're not getting that treasure.
I'm too swift for you!
Too slow!
Wait, come back!
Where are you going? Come back!
Noooo!
Aaaaagh!
Cut me down, or you'll never get the claw!
Where are you... get me down from this!
Sorry, but I have a treasure to get to...
So long "friend." Ha ha ha!
Get me out of here!
Hey, over here!
Don't leave me, for Arkay's sake!
Help me! Help!
Help me out of here!
Get me down. Get me down!
Ah, kill it. Kill it!
Get it away from me. Get it away!
Don't let it get me. Help!
Agh. Agh!
Keep it away from me!
Is it over?
Is it dead?
Is it safe now?
You'll never get the claw!
Too slow!
That's it. Keep hacking at it.
You did it. You killed it. Now cut me down before anything else shows up.
What? Who are you? Oh, never mind. Cut me down before that thing gets us!
The... the books? Oh, I see. I thought perhaps... Well, I thought you'd come for me.
But yes, the books. The Caller will have them. She was most interested in one of the volumes.
Although not interested enough to keep me from being locked up.
Oh, you've saved me! Thank you so much. Who knows what they'd have done to me if you hadn't come along.
I promise I'll help you get out of here.
Why did I ever come here?
Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can.
But I need your help. We need your help.
Occasionally. But an Orc blade is a very personal thing. Anger, regret, pride. All these things are put into our steel.
When my daughter Lash chose to leave the stronghold, I forged a sword to channel all my shame and grief.
Honor me with a task, outlander. Bring the sword to my daughter, if you see her. It'll be all she needs to know.
So that's it, then. "Don't come back."
Thank you. Here. You honored my mother's task and should be rewarded.
Now, I need to be alone.
Listen to your friend, Fruki. They are just dreams, my dear. I assure you that it is quite normal.
Everyone, please. I'm doing what I can to end these nightmares.
In the meantime all I ask is you remain strong and put your trust in Lady Mara.
The shrine of Namira is further inside. Keep going.
Draugr are below. Foul tasting.
You've returned. What happened in the Hall of the Dead?
Find out what's happening in the Hall of the Dead. Arkay will watch over you.
Tread lightly in the Hall. We don't know what's in there.
It's all right. I will not shun you for what you are. Stay. I will tell you everything you have forgotten.
A lot of our kind block out the memory of their first meal. The shame is too much. But you don't need to hide anymore.
Namira, the Lady of Decay, accepts you for what you are. She has a place for us, where we can sate our appetites without judgment.
To Oblivion with you then!
A priest, filled with the taste of an easy life. Brother Verulus, from Markarth.
Give him this gold. Tell him you need Arkay's help exploring an old cavern for treasure.
And when he stands in Namira's presence, she will take care of the rest.
I will be waiting at Reachcliff.
Say hello to Verulus for me.
The shrine of Namira is beyond that door. Get ready.
We're not done here.
"...and unwavering obedience to the officers of his great Empire."
"May those above judge me, and those below take me, if I fail in my duty."
"Long live the Emperor! Long live the Empire!"
Welcome to the Imperial Legion, soldier.
Just remember, we take care of our own. Once you're in the Legion, you're in it for life.
Speak to Beirand, he's normally out by the forge. He'll get you outfitted.
Now, if I'm not mistaken. Legate Rikke has a special assignment for you.
I've already said too much. If anyone overhears what we're saying, it could start a panic.
I would simply ask that you trust me and help me end Dawnstar's nightmares.
I'm concerned that when this place is unsealed, the Miasma will dissipate and they'll awaken; both orcs and priests alike.
The Miasma was created by the priests of Vaermina for their rituals. It's a gas that places the affected in a deep sleep.
Because the rituals would last for months or even years, the Miasma was designed to slow down the aging process.
Sadly, yes. The longer an individual is exposed to the Miasma, the more the mind can become damaged.
Those who've been under the effect of it for extended periods of time have been known to lose their minds entirely.
In some cases, a few never awoke at all.
I'm a strict follower of the old traditions. Honor, strength, deeds.
Malacath protect you.
"...Jarl of Windhelm and true High King of Skyrim."
"As Talos is my witness, may this oath bind me to death and beyond..."
"...even to my lord as to my fellow brothers and sisters in arms."
Now you're one of us.
Which means you get to tag along on a little trip with me.
Oh, and here. You're a Stormcloak now, you ought to look the part.
How quickly you've advanced. Most impressive.
There are spells yet to be learned, powerful spells that can more closely and more permanently bind creatures to your will.
But there are risks. There are always risks. You must summon and command an unbound Dremora.
You dare bring me here? You must be punished.
That's right! Don't worry, Uncle Ralof, I won't let those soldiers sneak up on you.
Here's a key to the house. Stay as long as you like. If there's anything else you need, just let me know.
Don't worry about me. I know how to lay low.
Helgen? Has something happened...?
What is it, woman? Sven drunk on the job again?
I'm glad to assist you in any way I can.
But I need your help. We need your help.
Hod. Just come here.
Hush, Frodnar. This is no time for your games.
Aw, mama, I want to stay and talk with Uncle Ralof!
Need a trained war dog? Fiercest companion money can buy.
I'll be handling the horses with Cedran gone. Miss that old man.
I like the loyalty of a dog. Not as fickle as a wolf.
You have to train a good war dog from a pup. Bite your arm clean off otherwise.
Good. Here you are. He'll follow you to Oblivion and back, if it comes to that.
He had a way of enthralling people and built up a loyal army of followers. Good men fell under his spell and were forever twisted. Made... evil.
After that, fear was enough to keep them in line. He could torture and humiliate them and they would not rebel. Even in death, they serve him still.
Hevnoraak planned his resurrection obsessively before dying. My duty in life and in death is to make sure he stays in this tomb for eternity.
Did you find all of the vessels?
Do not interrupt the ritual. Go empty the vessels and sit on the throne.
Hevnoraak is defeated.
I've told you a hundred times, I lock that chest for a reason. I don't want you getting a head full of ideas about adventure and glory.
It pleases me to know that I can still be of use even in the twilight of my life.
Hey, people have paid money for stranger things. Take our cow dung - we sell that by the wagon load.
I'll tell you who I don't envy - those poor children who lost their parents in this war.
You smell like a pig's pen, too. What a stink! Have you been rolling in the dirt all day?
You're right, it's none of your business. How about you keep your nose out of it?
But I don't want to be safe! I'm not afraid of the dangers out there. The only thing I'm afraid of is wasting my life on this farm.
Oh! Are you going to teach me how to turn into a mud crab?
Maybe you're right. Even so, I can't help wondering if some other force it at work here.
It's too fine a day to dwell on such things. Let's get back to work and forget about wars and orphans for awhile.
I just wanted to try on your old armor, from back when you were a soldier. I thought I might be old enough to wear it.
Now, clear your mind and breathe deeply. Good concentration is very important for spellcasting.
Well done! I don't know where I'd be without you, my old friend.
There's times I think you're the coldest man in Skyrim. This is your own flesh and blood you're talking about.
Another fine day at work in the fields. I don't envy those city folk who miss out on the joys of working the land.
I am not! You're lying!
I'm not saying it's my business, but I have to ask. Why are you so cruel to your little girls?
I know where this is going, son. As I've said many a time. the world is a dangerous place. You're much safer here at the farm, with me.
Today I'll teach you a useful spell that might just save your life. If you should ever fall into a river, you'll be very glad you learned it.
Luck? I think it has more to do with the hard work and good soil.
I was lucky. If you hadn't rescued me, I'd probably be a thief, a mercenary or something even worse.
Hah! And what would a witless oaf like you know about raising children?
I heard you sneaking around downstairs last night. Were you trying to break into my chest again, son? I've told you to leave it alone.
Wow! Then I could dive into a river and swim so far away that Britte and father could never find me.
The last wheat shipment fetched a fine price at market.
Maybe we should start selling the soil along with the dung. We could make a tidy profit.
They've grown, all right - lazier and more stupid.
I'm old enough to decide how to live my life, and I'd be honored to wear your armor, if you'd just let me. I'm strong enough to handle it.
Look at you! You're as filthy as a pig in a pen!
When you're rich, you can waste as much money as you like on far-fetched business ideas. In the meantime, there's a farm to run.
Nothing refreshes the soul like an honest day's labor in good, tilled earth.
Looks like it's going to be a bountiful harvest once again. The gods continue to smile upon us.
It's remarkable that our farms are so prosperous given the harsh winters of late.
Father, how old were you when you left home?
Looks like we're in for quite a harvest. We've had better luck with our planting than any village in the hold.
Lives lost, crops burned, land ruined... there's no greater sin than war. And yet, you were an orphan, and you've made something of your life.
You're a liar! I won't listen to you. Leave me alone!
Yes, that's your mother's side of the family talking. Just stay on for one more season, that's all I ask.
Oh, it's much better than that. I will teach you how to breathe underwater, with no need to turn into one of those ugly creatures.
The way our crops have been growing, Rorikstead must have the richest soil in the hold.
Go. Bring honor to yourself, and the Companions.
We've gotten a letter about someone needing some muscle over in the Reach.
Walkin' never hurt nobody. If you change your mind, I'll be here.
Oh. Was it something I said? I can't afford to lower the price, but come back if you change your mind.
Wish I did, but some outlander bought my last one.
That's what I'm here for. What are you offering?
Don't have any that are for sale right now.
Yes! Yes, you can. She's all saddled up and everything.
Good enough for me. It's the one with the saddle.
That would be a fair price. When you've got it, come see me.
Wow! Didn't think I could get that for her. I mean, sure!
Looks like you're a little short on coin though. Sorry.
Before we enter, I must warn you about the dangers that could be lurking within.
Years ago, this temple was raided by an orc war party seeking revenge... they were being plagued by nightmares just like the people of Dawnstar.
No. Knowing they could never defeat the orcs, the priests of Vaermina released what they call "The Miasma," putting everyone to sleep.
Arkay watch over you.
I'm just not up for it, sorry. I'd rather stay here, have a glass of wine, and perform my rites to Arkay in peace.
The meal is on Namira's table. Go ahead. Carve.
Come with me. Our feast is about to begin.
I need to lay down. I'll just be a minute....
Why don't you lay down and rest, while we get the meal ready?
I've been invited to dinner.... I'm so hungry....
Yes. I'm your friend, and I've invited you to dinner.
You know, in the Priesthood of Arkay, we have a joke about an empty pocket being better than a full grave.
Savos. Before... before he died.
You were told to find it, weren't you?
If the stories about the staff are true, if it really can absorb a tremendous amount of power, maybe we can break through Ancano's magic.
I said let's go. Now.
He... He gave me something just a little while ago.
He told me it was from Labyrinthian, and that I would know what to do with it when the time came.
I think... I think he meant this for you, then. I'm not sure why, but there was something very personal about it for him.
Also, I think you should take this amulet. It belonged to Savos, but I think would do you the most good now.
Take it, and get out of here. Bring back that staff before Ancano brings the whole College down around us.
Aghh..
Cicero can talk for a moment. Just a moment. Then Mother must be... tended to.
Oh, I do wish to get to know you better. But I must tend to mother. Maybe we can talk for just a moment...
Gods... the pain...
You'll... pay for this...
Leave me alone.
I'm trying to pay attention.
Cicero is at your service, oh great and powerful Listener!
Let's kill someone.
Oh yes, it is Cicero! You were a fool to spare me. What, did you think I would be grateful? Cicero should be Listener! Not you! Now you will die!
Ahhh.... gotcha! Oh, Listener, you should see the look on your face! Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha!
Oh, Cicero has returned! Not to kill the kind Listener, but to serve, until one of us dies horribly, in service to our Mother! Best friends forever!
In the meantime, I'll make myself at home in the Sanctuary. I'm sure Mother needs... tending.
Listener!
Humble Cicero lives to serve.
I just cook, and clean, and do whatever it is they ask of me.
I can't even keep track of all the people who have been in and out of this fort. They come, they go. I barely notice.
Hard enough to keep up with who owns this fort. One side takes over, the other runs away. I tell them, "I ain't leaving! I come with the place!" Ha!
I remember one morning, long time ago, I woke up and the place was run by some Orcs. Went to bed that night, it was all vampires. Don't matter to me.
Now, then. I been gabbin' long enough. Too much cleanin' to do.
You must excuse me. I shouldn't be here.
It is good to meet you in person.
Please, we do not have much time.

And so you fulfilled your destiny, which you once said you did not believe in.
But I cannot celebrate his fall. Zu'u tiiraaz ahst ok mah. He was my brother once. This world will never be the same.
So, it is done. Alduin dilon. The Eldest is no more, he who came before all others, and has always been.
Perhaps. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein.
And, as you told me once, the next world will have to take care of itself. Ful nii los. Even I cannot see past Time's ending.
I am glad you believe that. At least it will continue to exist. Grik los lein.
Even I cannot see past Time's ending to what comes next. Niid koraav zeim dinoksetiid. We must do the best we can with this world.
As for myself, you've proven your mastery twice over. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly acknowledge the power of your Thu'um.
And so Alduin's lordship passes to you. Thuri, Dovahkiin. I gladly submit to your mastery of the Thu'um.
But I forget myself. Krosis. So los mid fahdon. Melancholy is an easy trap for a dovah to fall into.
You have won a mighty victory. Sahrot krongrah - one that will echo through all the ages of this world for those who have eyes to see.
Savor your triumph, Dovahkiin. This is not the last of what you will write upon the currents of Time.
Happy? No, I am not happy. Zeymahi lost ont du'ul Bormahu. Alduin was once the crown of our father Akatosh's creation.
Zu'u Odahviing. Call me when you have need, and I will come if I can.
Perhaps now you have some insight into the forces that shape the vennesetiid... the currents of Time.
Perhaps you begin to see the world as a dovah.
Of course. Alduin wahlaan daanii. I would not have helped you if I thought otherwise.
You once told me you did not believe in destiny.
Indeed, you saw more clearly than I - certainly more clearly than Alduin. Rok funta koraav.
You did what was necessary. Alduin had flown far from the path of right action in his pahlok - the arrogance of his power.
Indeed.  Alduin wahlaan daanii.
His doom was written when he claimed for himself the lordship that properly belongs to Bormahu - our father Akatosh.

Namira grants us victory.

Feel free to stay and mingle with your new coven, if you have the strength.
I must return to Markarth.

I can smell the blood. Good and sour.
Good.

I love this part of the meal.

Tilma will keep the place clean. She always has.

Good people of Solitude.

Are you all enjoying yourselves? Please, eat, drink, and make merry on this most happy of days.

Aiiiieee!
Aiiiieee!

Aggh!

Arrggh!

No mercy!
Your end is near!
Out of my way!
You mean nothing!

It's good we hold the Reach. Were the rebels to gain control, the silver mines would give them coin to hire more thugs and expand their violence.
We need to stem the flow of silver to the rebellion. Until we retake the Reach, the Stormcloaks will plunder it's mines to fund further violence.
Hjaalmarch is an important buffer zone between the rebel forces and the capital. Ulfric will try to break through at some point.
I'm losing my patience with this damn war. We must take Hjaalmarch back! The Stormcloaks will be beating upon the doors of the capital soon.
Ulfric will want to take Whiterun from us. But we will hold fast. We can't give the rebellion free reign through the center of Skyrim.
I can't believe we lost Whiterun. The rebels have a great advantage in controlling the center now. We must take it back!
By holding onto Falkreath, we control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. Ulfric will try for it soon. We'll be there to stop him.
I fear losing Falkreath may give too much advantage to Ulfric. The rebels now control access to the Reach, Whiterun, and the Rift. I want it back.
Taking the Pale gives us another port in Dawnstar, and puts us within striking distance of Windhelm. That should make Ulfric a bit more cautious, eh?
Controlling the Pale gives Ulfric a port town and takes the pressure off of Windhelm. But it's only a matter of time before it's back in our hands.
Now that we have Winterhold, I can garrison some troops there until I'm ready to march on Windhelm.
There's not much up there, but if we can regain Winterhold, our proximity to Windhelm should keep Ulfric wary and wasting troops garrisoned at home.
I almost can't believe it's over. The rebellion is squashed, Windhelm is free. I must admit, Ulfric put up a stronger fight than I was expecting.
We will oust Ulfric from Windhelm soon enough. And when we do, I'll have his head on a plate.
Taking control of the Rift was a great victory. Ulfric must be rather nervous with us so close to his door step.
We need the Rift. We need to regain control of it before we can march on Windhelm without worry about our rear guard.
Soon enough we will march on Solitude. And on that day the heroes of Sovngarde will march with us, and the Empire will fall to its knees in fear.
Now that the Empire has been driven from the Reach we can put a stop to the raping of her silver mines. That silver belongs in Skyrim.
The Empire's ravenous hunger for Skyrim's silver makes them fierce. But rest assured, we'll win the Reach and wrest it from their greedy paws.
Tullius must be getting nervous. Taking Hjaalmarch we're practically in his backyard now. As soon as we're able, we'll march on Solitude.
We should take Hjaalmarch. It'll be an important pressure point on the Imperials. We'd be within spitting distance of the capital.
We've driven the Imperials out of Whiterun. This is good. Very good. We now control the center. It's a powerful position. One I aim to keep.
The Imperials have really dug into Whiterun. We'll wrest it from them soon enough. We must. Its central position is key.
I'm proud we liberated Falkreath from the Empire. In many ways it's the heart and soul of Skyrim. Not to mention its rather strategic position.
The Empire thinks it holds Falkreath from us. But the souls of Skyrim's bravest are buried there and will fill our hearts and strengthen our blades.
Tullius has camps in the Pale, entertaining thoughts of battle. The Empire still underestimates the force of our cause. It will be their undoing.
I can't believe we let them take the Pale. Losing the port of Dawnstar is lessened only by the threat of Imperial forces being so close to Windhelm.
I'm not sure why Tullius is wasting his time trying to take Winterhold from us. But if he wants to throw his men away, I'll gladly accept that gift.
I don't like Imperial troops holed up in Winterhold... such a close march to Windhelm. So I'm sending a campaign up there to take it back.
Damn Tullius. He's pocketed men in the Rift. It's dangerous having Imperials skulking about on the southern border of Eastmarch.
Losing the Rift gives Tullius easy access to the south of Eastmarch. Talos preserve us. We must take it back!
By defeating Ulfric at Whiterun we have solidified the allegiance of the Jarl.
Perhaps the Stormcloaks will soon lose heart for this little rebellion of theirs once and for all.

Looks like I chose the right person for the job.

Oh, yes. Presiding over the union of two lovers is one of my greatest responsibilities. It helps that Asgeir and Vittoria truly care for each other.
Skjor was looking for you before. Talk to him before doing anything else.

Do dogs count?

That must have been quite a surprise.

"The bloodiest beef in the Reach!" Ha ha ha.
Not too full on bread and wine, I hope?
Praise to you, champion!
Ah, the new disciple. I hear you have quite the appetite.

Now this is going to be a good feast.
Of course they don't count!
With good planning and constant vigilance.

Until brave heroes step forward to destroy these monsters, that is the best we can do.

You'll want the Bannered Mare. See old Hulda, she'll keep you swimmin' in mead.

My brother Jon had the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war. Too bad he died before he had the chance.
I...I miss our son terribly, especially since Eorlund died. Thorald... he went missing, fighting the Imperials.
Eorlund Gray-Mane was a stubborn fool. Our friendship ended when he turned his back on the Emperor. Now he's dead.
My wife was a good woman. She could be a little hard-headed sometimes, but I loved her just the same. Gods I miss her.
I don't claim to be the best blacksmith in Whiterun. Eorlund Gray-Mane was that and more, but now he's dead. All I ask is a fair chance.

My father never thought I was smart enough to be a stablemaster. I just wish he was around to see me now.
Time to fight!
Finally! Let's end this.

You're nothing but a joke!

I'll give no quarter!

That was fun.
Waste of time.
Didn't even work up a sweat.
Pathetic.

Help!

No offense, I mean I'm sure you aren't into any of that kind of stuff.

My aunt went in after him, but she hasn't come out yet and I don't think I can take him by myself.

You might consider putting on... something. Anything.

Gonna get cold tonight. Might want to at least cover your delicates.
Please. Remove your naked obscenity from the company of civilized folk.
Dear. You really ought to put some clothes on. You're embarrassing yourself.
Enough already. Put on some clothes. You're making people nervous.
You're naked!

Dress yourself.

Cover yourself. Have you no shame?
Next time, you might want to get dressed before leaving the house.
This one suggests donning some apparel.
Ysmir's beard, you're going to freeze to death...
Put on some damn clothes already!
You really should put on some clothes, you know.

It's probably not a good idea to walk around naked like that.
It should put on some coverings.
You're naked!
Put on some clothes, you damned fool.
You ought to cover your... unmentionables.
